Items.Title: 用 CSS 重新创建 1883 年的日本烟花目录 | Re-creating a Japanese Fireworks Catalog from 1883 in CSS
Items.Description:
麦兹·斯托曼

发布于 7 月 5 日

有一天，我收到了作者 Robin Sloan 发来的时事通讯，其中包含 1883 年日本烟花目录中一些美丽的旧页面的样本：

这恰逢 Codepen 的每周挑战“烟花”——所以我不得不尝试重新创造这些美丽的事物。

这就是我最终得到的——我自己对第 66 项和第 67 项混合的解释：

其中涉及到很多小而有趣的东西，我想分享！

颗粒状叠加

为了获得粗糙、颗粒状的外观，我添加了覆盖整个页面的覆盖层 - 它位于<body>标签上的::after上，并使用Grainy Gradients Playground制作。

小星星/爆炸

较小的星星/爆炸使用的是clip-path ，是用我自己的工具“多边形到剪辑路径和 SVG”制作的 - 您可以在 Codepen 上找到它：

这个工具对我来说具有很大的情感价值，因为原始算法是由我的一位亲爱的朋友编写的，他于 2020 年不幸去世。

大星/爆炸

主要爆炸由一个grid组成，所有项目都放置在中心，使用grid-column: 1/-1和grid-row: 1/-1 。 这是新的position: absolute ，但增加了灵活性。 每个<b>子节点都有自己的--deg （度数）属性，总共 36 个，它们之间的度数为 10。

在这些之上，我放置了一个repeating-conic-gradient蒙版，以“剪掉” <b>的内部堆栈：

 --mask: repeating-conic-gradient( from 45deg at 50% 50%, var(--red) 0deg 7.5deg, transparent 7.5deg 10deg);

谷歌字体 API

我使用两种字体，一种用于数字，一种用于＆符号。 加载整个字符集完全浪费带宽，所以幸运的是 Google Fonts 支持使用 URL 中的text参数仅指定您需要的字符：

 /* Numero Sign and Numbers */ @import url('fonts.googleapis.com ... &text=№67'); /* Other font, Ampersand */ @import url('fonts.googleapis.com ... &text=%26');

口罩和其他东西

  • 在黑色背景和带有数字的盒子上，我使用了在Open Props上找到的蒙版。

  • 我正在使用cqi -units（容器查询），因此它具有完全响应能力。

  • 我正在使用原生 CSS 嵌套，所以不要在 Firefox 中预览；-)

您可以在此处查看最终结果：

一旦解除暂停，madsstoumann 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，madsstoumann 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 madsstoumann 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 madsstoumann：

使 madsstoumann 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

原文:

Mads Stoumann

Posted on Jul 5

 

The other day I received a newsletter from the author Robin Sloan, containing samples of some beautiful old pages from a Japanese fireworks catalog from 1883:

This coincided with Codepen's weekly challenge being "Fireworks" — so I just had to try to re-create these beauties.

This is what I ended up with — my own interpretation of a mix between item № 66 and item № 67:

It involves a lot of small, interesting stuff, that I'd like to share!

Grainy Overlay

To get that rough, grainy look, I added an overlay covering the whole page — it's on ::after on the <body>-tag, and was made using Grainy Gradients Playground.

Small Stars/Explosions

The smaller stars/explosions are using clip-path, made with my own tool, "Polygon to clip-path and SVG" — you can find it on Codepen:

This tool has a lot of sentimental value to me, as the original algorithm was coded by a dear friend of mine, who sadly passed away in 2020.

Large Star/Explosion

The main explosion consists of a grid with all items being placed center, using grid-column: 1/-1 and grid-row: 1/-1. It's the new position: absolute for me, but with added flexibility. Each <b>-sub-node has it's own --deg(degree)-property, a total of 36 with a 10 degree between them.

On top of these, I've placed a mask with a repeating-conic-gradient to "cut out" the inner stacks of <b>s:

--mask: repeating-conic-gradient( from 45deg at 50% 50%, var(--red) 0deg 7.5deg, transparent 7.5deg 10deg);

Google Fonts API

I'm using two fonts, one for the numbers and one for the ampersand. It would be a complete waste of bandwidth to load the entire character-sets, so luckily Google Fonts support specifying only the characters you need, using the text-parameter in the URL:

/* Numero Sign and Numbers */
@import url('fonts.googleapis.com ... &text=№67');
/* Other font, Ampersand */
@import url('fonts.googleapis.com ... &text=%26');

Masks and other stuff

  • On the black background and on the box with numbers, I use a mask, I found on Open Props.

  • I'm using cqi-units (container-queries), so it's fully responsive.

  • I'm using native CSS nesting, so don't preview in Firefox ;-)

You can see the final result here:

Items.Title: 使用 React 服务器组件和服务器操作构建 2000 年代的留言簿 | Build a Guestbook from the 2000s with React Server Components and Server Actions
Items.Description:
如今，React 正在蓬勃发展。 尽管它是作为客户端 UI 库创建的，但它现在可用于从服务器生成几乎所有内容。 我们从这一变化中获益匪浅，尤其是与 Next.js 结合使用时。

在这个简短的教程中，我们将使用React 服务器组件和服务器操作来构建 00 年代早期的东西：留言簿！

我使用 TypeScript 和应用程序路由器引导了 Next.js 应用程序。 因为我不想关心 CSS，所以我使用了Water.css 。 为了存储消息，我使用了Vercel KV （这可能不是适合此类用例的工具，但对于本文的目的来说已经足够了）。 演示项目的完整源代码可在 GitHub 上获取。

从服务器组件获取消息

让我们从显示现有消息开始。 使用经典的 React 组件，我们必须声明本地状态，在useEffect中执行fetch请求等。使用 Next.js 的服务器端渲染，我们可以声明一个getServerSideProps函数来首先获取消息。

使用服务器组件，我们可以从组件本身获取消息：

 export async function MessageList() { const messages = await fetchMessages() return ( <> <h2>Last Messages</h2> {messages.map(({ id, name, content, date }) => ( <blockquote key={id}> <div>{content}</div> <small>{`–${name}, ${formatDate(date)}`}</small> </blockquote> ))} </> ) }

你觉得很奇怪吗？ 它应该！ 该组件被声明为async ，并从其主体中获取数据，没有任何useEffect ？

这就是服务器组件让我们做的事情。 由于服务器组件只能在服务器上呈现，因此我们可以执行异步操作，例如查询数据库、发出 HTTP 请求，甚至从文件系统读取。

因此，请注意该组件如何易于阅读，即使是像您一样不熟悉 React 的人也是如此。 我们获取数据并显示它。 就是这样。

如果获取消息需要几秒钟怎么办？ 这是否意味着页面需要花费那么多时间来加载？ 幸运的是，没有。 我们可以利用 React 的Suspense在组件仍在获取数据时显示一条漂亮的加载消息。

在显示消息的页面中，我们可以使用Suspense包装MessageList组件，并带有后备消息。

 export default function MessagesPage() { return ( <> {/* ... */} <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading messages…</p>}> <MessageList /> </Suspense> </> ) }

要更深入地了解服务器组件和 Suspense，请查看我之前的文章使用 React 服务器组件在您的博客上显示视图计数器。

这就是我们从服务器组件读取数据的方式，但是是否有类似的方法可以写入数据，以便我们可以轻松地发布新消息？

使用服务器操作提交表单

服务器组件让我们无需使用useEffect设置流程即可获取数据。 借助服务器操作，我们可以执行类似从表单提交数据的操作。

让我们从这个基本形式开始：

 export function MessageForm() { return ( <form> <h2>New Message</h2> <label htmlFor="name">Your name:</label> <input type="text" id="name" name="name" required minLength={3} /> <label htmlFor="content">Your message:</label> <textarea id="content" name="content" required minLength={3} /> <button type="submit">Send</button> </form> ) }

正如您所看到的，这里没有什么特别的：两个字段、一个按钮和一些验证属性。

要提交此表格，我们通常有两种选择：

  • 使用action属性来使用处理表单数据的 API 端点，
  • 手动发出fetch请求以调用此 API 端点。

在第一种情况下，当用户提交表单时页面将重新加载，这是我们可能希望避免的情况。 第二种场景是我们在 React 应用程序中更喜欢的场景，但设置起来有点复杂。

通过服务器操作，我们可以两全其美。 我们可以创建一个函数，当提交表单时，该函数将在服务器上调用：

 async function submitMessageForm(formData: FormData) { 'use server' await postMessage({ content: formData.get('content'), name: formData.get('name'), }) }

通过在函数体中添加'use server' ，我们将其声明为服务器操作。 然后我们可以使用action属性将其与表单关联起来：

 return ( <form action={submitMessageForm}>

注意：要在 Next.js 项目中使用服务器操作，您需要在next.config.js文件中启用它们：

 experimental: { serverActions: true },

表单数据验证怎么样？ 我们在表单本身上声明了一些验证属性，但一如既往，它并不妨碍我们验证服务器上的数据：

 // Validation example using a Zod schema const message = formDataSchema.parse({ content: formData.get('content'), name: formData.get('name'), }) await postMessage(message)

最后，我们可以对我们的服务器操作进行另一个很好的改进。 通过添加对 Next.js 的revalidatePath函数的调用，我们可以告诉应用程序某些资源需要重新加载。 换句话说，我们可以在创建新消息后刷新页面上显示的消息：

 await postMessage(message) revalidatePath('/') // assuming the messages are displayed at root

这是我们的表单现在的样子：

我们可以看到消息已添加到列表中，无需刷新页面。 但是表单提交后并没有重置，这可能有点烦人。

此外，您可能注意到表单验证非常简单：我们使用 HTML 验证属性进行客户端验证，并使用 Zod 模式进行服务器验证。 如果双方使用相同的验证该有多好？

好消息：并不是因为我们使用服务器操作而不能使用很酷的客户端功能来处理表单。 让我们看看如何。

利用客户端功能改进表单

到目前为止，我们的表单没有使用任何客户端功能。 实际上，它仍然是一个服务器组件（可以在禁用 JavaScript 的情况下使用）。 要为其添加一些很酷的功能，我们首先将其设为客户端组件。

这意味着：

  • 在文件顶部添加'use client'指令，
  • 将服务器操作移动到新的actions.ts文件中，因为我们无法在客户端文件中声明服务器操作。

为了改进我们的表单，我将使用react-hook-form库。 以下是如何在我们的表单上使用它：

 export function MessageForm() { const { register, handleSubmit } = useForm<MessageFormData>({ shouldUseNativeValidation: true, resolver: zodResolver(formDataSchema), }) return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit((data) => submitMessageForm(data))}> <h2>New Message</h2> <label htmlFor="name">Your name:</label> <input type="text" id="name" {...register('name')} /> <label htmlFor="content">Your message:</label> <textarea id="content" {...register('content')} /> <button type="submit">Send</button> </form> ) }

通过在表单的每个字段上使用register ，我们让库处理表单本地状态和验证。 注意：

  • 我们使用现有的 Zod 模式来定义验证规则（感谢React-hook-form的Zod 解析器），
  • 通过设置shouldUseNativeValidation: true ，我们依靠浏览器 API 在表单中显示验证错误（我喜欢的功能！）。

我们需要对服务器操作进行一些更改，以符合react-hook-form（一个普通对象而不是FormData对象）发送数据的新方式：

 export async function submitMessageForm(formData: MessageFormData) { formDataSchema.parse(formData) await postMessage(formData) revalidatePath('/') }

重要提示：我们可能认为因为我们直接从表单调用函数，所以不必关心验证表单数据（参数）。 我们确实需要关心它！ 尽管 Next.js 为我们处理了它，但仍然会向 API 端点发出 HTTP 请求来调用服务器操作。 使用无效数据手动进行此调用非常容易。

这就是为什么我们仍然在函数顶部检查数据是否符合 Zod 模式，即使 TypeScript 让我们认为数据已经是MessageFormData 。 但至少，我们可以在客户端和服务器上使用相同的验证逻辑。

所以现在我们可以更好地处理验证。 我们甚至还可以改进形式。

首先，我们在提交表单后重置表单。 最后的改进：如果提交需要几秒钟，最好告诉用户表单正在提交，并防止他们再次单击提交按钮。 再次， react-hook-form提供了我们需要的信息： isSubmitting标志：

 export function MessageForm() { const { // ... formState: { isSubmitting }, } = useForm<MessageFormData>(/* ... */) return ( {/* ... */} <button type="submit" disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send'} </button> {/* ... */} ) }

现在，由于客户端功能，我们在客户端和服务器上以相同的方式处理验证，我们告诉用户表单正在提交，并在完成后重置表单。 微小的改进，但对于用户体验和开发人员体验来说却很棒。

这是我们最终的留言簿的样子：

公平地说，我们最后所做的这些改进是有代价的：如果没有启用 JavaScript，就不可能再使用该表单。 我会让你决定是否值得。

那么，最后，服务器操作为我们提供了什么，它们与服务器组件相比如何？

  • 服务器组件让我们无需创建额外的端点并从客户端调用它即可获取数据，
  • 服务器操作让我们可以发布数据，而无需创建额外的端点并从客户端调用它。

我喜欢服务器组件和操作的地方在于它们隐藏了一些逻辑，而不产生任何魔力，并且使代码方式更容易理解。 对我来说，它们甚至让学习 React 变得更容易，这是我在文章《使用服务器组件进行 React 的更好学习路径》中提出的观点。

从长远来看，说它们将改变我们构建 Web 应用程序的方式还为时过早，但我愿意赌！



原文:

React is living something these days. Although it was created as a client UI library, it can now be used to generate almost everything from the server. And we get a lot from this change, especially when coupled with Next.js.

In this short tutorial, we’ll use React Server Components and Server Actions to build something from the early '00s: a guestbook!

I bootstrapped a Next.js app with TypeScript and the app router. As I don’t want to care about the CSS, I used Water.css. And to store the messages, I went with Vercel KV (which may not be the right tool for this kind of use case, but is plenty enough for the purpose of this post). The complete source code of the demo project is available on GitHub.

Fetching the messages from a Server Component

Let’s start with displaying the existing messages. With a classic React component, we’d have to declare a local state, perform a fetch request in a useEffect, etc. With Next.js’ server-side rendering, we could declare a getServerSideProps function to fetch the messages first.

With server components, we can fetch the messages from the component itself:

export async function MessageList() { const messages = await fetchMessages() return ( <> <h2>Last Messages</h2> {messages.map(({ id, name, content, date }) => ( <blockquote key={id}> <div>{content}</div> <small>{`–${name}, ${formatDate(date)}`}</small> </blockquote> ))} </> )
}

Looks odd to you? It should! The component is declared async, and fetches data from its body, without any useEffect?

This is what server components let us do. As a server component can be rendered only on the server, we can perform asynchronous operations such as querying a database, making HTTP requests, or even read from the filesystem.

As a consequence, notice how the component is easy to read, even by someone who’s not as familiar with React as you are. We fetch the data, and we display it. That’s it.

What if fetching the messages takes a few seconds? Does it mean that the page will take that much time to load? Fortunately, no. We can take advantage of React’s Suspense to display a nice loading message while the component is still fetching the data.

In the page displaying messages, we can wrap our MessageList component with Suspense, with a fallback message.

export default function MessagesPage() { return ( <> {/* ... */} <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading messages…</p>}> <MessageList /> </Suspense> </> )
}

For a deeper dive in Server Components and Suspense, have a look at my previous post Display a view counter on your blog with React Server Components.

So this is how we can read data from a server component, but is there something similar for writing data so we can post new messages as easily?

Submitting forms with Server Actions

Server components let us fetch data without having to set up a flow with useEffect. We can do something similar to submit data from a form, thanks to Server Actions.

Let’s start with this basic form:

export function MessageForm() { return ( <form> <h2>New Message</h2> <label htmlFor="name">Your name:</label> <input type="text" id="name" name="name" required minLength={3} /> <label htmlFor="content">Your message:</label> <textarea id="content" name="content" required minLength={3} /> <button type="submit">Send</button> </form> )
}

As you can see, there is nothing fancy here: two fields, a button, and some validation attributes.

To submit this form, we usually have two options:

  • use the action attribute to use an API endpoint handling the form data,
  • manually make a fetch request to call this API endpoint.

In the first scenario, the page would reload when the user submits the form, which is something we may want to avoid. The second scenario is the one we tend to prefer in React apps, but is a bit more complex to set up.

With server actions, we can get the best of both worlds. We can create a function that will be called on the server when the form is submitted:

async function submitMessageForm(formData: FormData) { 'use server' await postMessage({ content: formData.get('content'), name: formData.get('name'), })
}

By adding 'use server' in the function body, we declare it as a server action. We can then associate it with the form by using the action attribute:

return ( <form action={submitMessageForm}>

Note: to use server actions in a Next.js project, you need to enable them in your next.config.js file:

experimental: { serverActions: true },

What about form data validation? We declared some validation attributes on the form itself, but as always it doesn’t prevent us of validating the data on the server:

// Validation example using a Zod schema
const message = formDataSchema.parse({ content: formData.get('content'), name: formData.get('name'),
}) await postMessage(message)

Finally, we can make another nice improvement to our server action. By adding a call to Next.js’ revalidatePath function, we can tell the app that some resources need to be reloaded. Said differently, we can refresh the messages displayed on the page after we created the new one:

await postMessage(message)
revalidatePath('/') // assuming the messages are displayed at root

Here is what our form looks like now:

We can see that the message is added to the list without needing to refresh the page. But the form is not reset after being submitted, which can be a little annoying.

Additionally, you probably noticed that the form validation is quite minimalist: we use the HTML validation attributes for the client validation, and a Zod schema for the server one. How nice would it be to use the same validation on both sides?

Good news: it is not because we use server actions that we can’t use cool client features to handle forms. Let’s see how.

Improving the form with client features

So far, our form doesn’t use any client feature. Actually, it is still a server component (that can be used with JavaScript disabled). To add some cool features to it, let’s start by making it a client component.

This means:

  • adding the 'use client' directive at the top of the file,
  • moving the server action to a new actions.ts file, as we can’t declare server actions in client files.

To improve our form, I will use the react-hook-form library. Here is how to use it on our form:

export function MessageForm() { const { register, handleSubmit } = useForm<MessageFormData>({ shouldUseNativeValidation: true, resolver: zodResolver(formDataSchema), }) return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit((data) => submitMessageForm(data))}> <h2>New Message</h2> <label htmlFor="name">Your name:</label> <input type="text" id="name" {...register('name')} /> <label htmlFor="content">Your message:</label> <textarea id="content" {...register('content')} /> <button type="submit">Send</button> </form> )
}

By using register on each field of the form, we let the library handle the form local state and validation. Note that:

  • we use the existing Zod schema to define the validation rules (thanks to the Zod resolver for react-hook-form),
  • by setting shouldUseNativeValidation: true, we rely on the browser API to display the validation errors in the form (a feature I love!).

We need to change a bit the server action to comply with the new way data is sent by react-hook-form (a plain object instead of a FormData object):

export async function submitMessageForm(formData: MessageFormData) { formDataSchema.parse(formData) await postMessage(formData) revalidatePath('/')
}

Important note: we may think that because we call the function directly from the form, we don’t have to care about validating the form data (the parameter). We do need to care about it! Although Next.js handles it for us, there is still an HTTP request made to an API endpoint to call the server action. And it is very easy to make this call manually with invalid data.

This is why we still check that the data is conform with the Zod schema at the top of the function, even if TypeScript lets us think the data is already a MessageFormData. But at least, we can use the same validation logic both on the client and the server.

So now we handle the validation a bit better. We can still even improve the form.

First, let’s reset the form after it is submitted. react-hook-form provides a reset function that we can call right after calling the server action (which returns a promise):

export function MessageForm() { const { // ... reset, } = useForm<MessageFormData>(/* ... */) return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit(async (data) => { await submitMessageForm(data) reset() })} >

Final improvement: if the submission takes a couple of seconds, it is a good practice to tell the user the form is being submitted, and prevent them from clicking the submit button again. And again, react-hook-form provides the information we need: an isSubmitting flag:

export function MessageForm() { const { // ... formState: { isSubmitting }, } = useForm<MessageFormData>(/* ... */) return ( {/* ... */} <button type="submit" disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send'} </button> {/* ... */} )
}

Now, thanks to client features, we handle validation the same way on the client and the server, we tell the user the form is being submitted, and reset the form when it is done. Tiny improvements but great for the user experience, as well as the developer experience.

Here is how our final guestbook looks like:

To be fair, these last improvements we made come with a cost: it isn’t possible anymore to use the form without having JavaScript enabled. I’ll let you decide whether it is worth it or not.

So, in the end, what do Server Actions offer us, and how do they compare to Server Components?

  • Server Components let us fetch data without creating an additional endpoint and calling it from the client,
  • Server Actions let us post data without creating an additional endpoint and calling it from the client.

What I love about server component and actions is that they hide some logic, without making anything magic, and make the code way easier to understand. To me, they even make it easier to learn React, a point of view I developed in my post A better learning path for React with server components.

It’s a bit early to say they will change the way we build web apps on the long term, but I would take that bet!

Items.Title: 数据库区域及其重要性 | Database regions and why they matter
Items.Description:
为Xata欢呼

发布于 7 月 5 日

为什么该地区很重要？

当选择正确的区域（支持数据库的底层基础设施所在的位置）时，您正在选择数据的存储位置及其处理方式。

出于多种原因，为您的数据库选择正确的区域至关重要：

  • 数据安全与合规：在数据世界中，每个国家和地区都有其独特的数据隐私和合规法规。 通过为您的数据库选择特定区域，您可以保证您的数据严格按照所需的安全措施和法律义务进行存储和处理。 您可以确保您的数据处理程序符合必要的标准和保障措施，让您在数据隐私和保护方面高枕无忧。

  • 性能优化：应用程序和数据库之间的物理距离会影响性能 - 邻近确实很重要！ 通过选择距离用户或应用程序服务器较近的区域，您可以最大限度地减少延迟并增强用户体验。 战略性地选择区域有助于提高速度并确保您的用户享受无缝且响应迅速的应用程序性能。

  • 容灾和冗余：跨多个区域的分布式数据库提供强大的容灾能力。 如果一个区域发生中断或中断，您的数据仍然可用并可从其他区域访问，从而确保连续性。

扩大 Xata 的覆盖范围

在 Xata，我们了解全球可访问性和可扩展性的重要性，以及开发人员在管理数据库时面临的挑战。 无论是美国东部还是西部、都柏林、法兰克福还是悉尼，开发人员都可以利用 Xata 可靠且可扩展的数据库服务。 这就是为什么我们很高兴地宣布，我们正在针对开发社区的一项最重要请求采取行动，并引入两个新的 Xata 区域：悉尼和法兰克福。

通过扩展我们的 Xata 区域以包括太平洋和欧洲的其他位置，我们的目标是为开发人员提供全面的解决方案，以满足他们的无服务器数据库需求。 无论您是要构建强大的电子商务平台、使用矢量搜索的人工智能应用程序还是高性能 API，我们的基础设施都已准备好满足您的需求。

随着我们继续扩大我们的产品范围，并正在筹备更多地区，令人兴奋的未来计划就在眼前。 我们的目标是不断增强 Xata 平台，使世界各地的开发人员和企业能够在无服务器环境中充分利用 PostgreSQL 数据库的潜力。 我们相信培育一个协作和支持的开发者社区，您的反馈对于塑造我们平台的未来至关重要。 感谢您的支持和参与，我们很高兴听到您与新地区合作的经历。 您的见解和观察对于帮助我们提供最佳体验至关重要。

如果您有任何问题、建议，或者只是想分享您的 Xata 故事，请随时通过Discord或Twitter与我们联系。

一旦暂停，xata 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，xata 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，xata 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 xata 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 xata：

使 xata 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 xata 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

joan-ing for Xata

Posted on Jul 5

 

Why the region matters?

When selecting the right region (the location where the underlying infrastructure supporting your database is located), you are choosing where your data is stored and how it is processed.

Selecting the right region for your database is crucial for a number of reasons:

  • Data security and compliance: In the world of data, each country and region has its unique set of data privacy and compliance regulations. By selecting a specific region for your database, you can guarantee that your data is stored and processed in strict accordance with the required security measures and legal obligations. You can ensure your data handling procedures meet the necessary standards and safeguards, providing peace of mind regarding data privacy and protection.

  • Performance Optimization: The physical distance between your application and the database can impact performance - proximity does matter! By selecting a region that is closer to your users or application servers, you can minimize latency and enhance user experiences. Strategically choosing a region can help boost speeds and ensure your users enjoy a seamless and responsive application performance.

  • Disaster Recovery and Redundancy: Distributed databases across multiple regions provide robust disaster recovery capabilities. If one region experiences an outage or disruption, your data remains available and accessible from other regions, ensuring continuity.

Expanding Xata’s reach

At Xata, we understand the importance of global accessibility and scalability, as well as the challenges developers face when it comes to managing databases. Whether it's the Eastern or Western US, Dublin, Frankfurt, or Sydney, developers can leverage Xata’s reliable and scalable database services. That's why we're thrilled to announce that we're taking action on one of our dev community’s top requests and are introducing the addition of two new Xata regions: Sydney and Frankfurt.

By extending our Xata regions to include additional locations in the Pacific and Europe, we aim to offer developers a comprehensive solution for their serverless database needs. Whether you're building a robust e-commerce platform, an AI application using vector-search, or a high-performance API, our infrastructure is ready to support your requirements.

Exciting plans for the future lie ahead as we continue to expand our offerings, with additional regions in the pipeline. Our goal is to continually enhance the Xata platform, empowering developers and businesses worldwide to leverage the full potential of PostgreSQL databases in a serverless environment. We believe in fostering a collaborative and supportive developer community, and your feedback is essential in shaping the future of our platform. Your support and participation are appreciated, and we're excited to hear about your experiences working with the new regions. Your insights and observations are crucial in helping us provide the best possible experience.

If you have any questions, suggestions, or just want to share your Xata stories, please don't hesitate to get in touch with us either on Discord or Twitter.

Items.Title: 构建您的第一个编码项目并避免教程地狱 | Build Your First Coding Project and Avoid Tutorial Hell
Items.Description:
虽然“真正的”地狱可能存在，也可能不存在（这里不需要讨论宗教信仰），但教程地狱是非常真实的。

我对教程地狱的定义是这样的：

教程地狱是指你编写代码，别人向你解释如何编写，但当你面前一片空白时，你自己却不明白如何编写。

在某些时候，是时候卸下辅助轮并自己构建一些东西了。

什么时候构建自己的项目？

在Boot.dev ，学生在完成以下模块后编写他们的第一个非教程项目：

  • 学习Python（课程）
  • 学习 OOP（课程）
  • 构建 Python CLI（项目）
  • 学习算法（课程）
  • 学习数据结构（课程）
  • 构建迷宫求解器（项目）
  • 个人项目 1 （当他们构建第一个完全自定义项目时）

现在，请记住，Boot.dev 学生 100% 的时间都在使用键盘。 我并不是说您应该在构建任何东西之前观看 40 小时的视频，而是说您应该在构建自己的想法之前对编码基础知识有深入的了解。 您应该通过编写代码来学习这些基础知识，但最好是您正在处理的项目和挑战得到指导，这样您就可以更多地专注于学习概念，而不是弄清楚要构建什么。

您的第一个项目的目标是什么？

你的目标不是建造一些能赚钱的东西，甚至不是建造一些能出现在你的简历上的东西。

第一个项目的目标只是让您习惯从头开始构建某些东西的过程。 您只需要进行一些练习，将项目分解为更小的部分。 当你第一次从教程地狱的深处走出来时，你需要体验第一口新鲜空气。

您的第一个项目有哪些好的要求？

  • 尝试在这个项目上花费 20-40 个小时。
  • 使用您已经熟悉的编程语言。
  • 经常将代码提交到 Git，并将其推送到 Github。
  • 为您的项目创建README.md 文件。 它应该向读者解释：
    • 该项目是什么以及它的作用
    • 如何克隆并运行它
  • 可以（我什至鼓励）您使用第三方库！ 也就是说，请确保您编写的某些代码正在做一些有趣的事情。 您不希望第三方库的极薄包装器成为您编写的唯一内容。

但我应该构建什么？

个人项目的重点在于它是个人的。 这个想法必须是你的。 即使您现在构建的项目很简单，但至少它是您的。

构建一些您觉得有趣的东西。

这可能不是一个会出现在你的作品集或简历中的项目，但是养成构建有趣且有用的东西的习惯仍然是件好事。 有趣且有用的项目有很多好处：

  • 工作起来更有趣
  • 他们有更有趣和更困难的问题需要解决
  • 您（或其他人）可能实际上会使用它们
  • 他们更有可能吸引其他人的注意力
  • 其他人的关注可以带来职业机会

使用您至少已经有点熟悉的编程语言。 假设您没有在 Boot.dev 之外编写大量代码，您的项目可能应该是在命令行上运行的 Python 程序。 没关系！

我想提供一些想法来让你的创意源源不断，但请不要从这个列表中选择一个项目，至少要加入你自己的想法

  • 终端中基于文本的冒险游戏
  • 用于检测密码强度或验证密码的 CLI 工具
  • 从图像创建 ASCII 艺术的工具
  • 用于在终端中查看 Reddit 源的命令行工具
  • 一个命令行工具，可以解析 Pokemon 数据的 CSV 文件，并让您按名称、类型等搜索 Pokemon。

但是，但是，但是……我不知道如何构建“X”！

作为开发人员，你的工作就是不断构建你以前从未构建过的东西。 不要因为不确定从哪里开始而回避一个想法。 去谷歌上查询。 询问 ChatGPT。 询问 Discord 社区。 您可能需要花一个小时的时间进行研究，然后才能开始确定您的想法在 40 小时的时间内是否可行，但这没关系！

假设你会做一些你以前从未做过的事情！ 你已经掌握了一些基础知识。 现在是时候将所有内容拼凑起来并进行研究了。

你需要帮助

你会被卡住的。 如果你没有陷入困境，那么你可能选择了一个太简单的想法。

诀窍是培养一套摆脱困境的技能。 这项技能将在您作为开发人员的职业生涯中为您提供良好的帮助（并且绝对必要）。

习惯谷歌搜索

从谷歌开始。 对你的想法做一些研究。 看看其他人在构建类似项目时做了什么。 看看是否有任何可以安装的库来让您的生活更轻松。

使用ChatGPT

讨论您使用 ChatGPT（橡皮鸭调试）的问题可能是摆脱困境的好方法。

不要让 ChatGPT 为您编写代码！ 你不会那样学习，而且如果你陷入这种习惯，你的学习速度会非常慢。 相反，使用 ChatGPT 来帮助您思考问题，并要求它尝试查找代码中的错误或解释您不理解的代码片段。

请记住，ChatGPT 可能确实是错误的，因此不要将其言论视为法律。 使用它可以回到正轨，但要通过其他来源验证它为您提供的信息。

使用不和谐

在Boot.dev Discord 服务器中提问，特别是如果您是当前的学生（但无论如何都欢迎您）！ 与所有提问一样，请提供尽可能多的背景信息，以便当您的同学和导师提供帮助时，他们可以有效地为您提供所需的信息。

发布和分享

将您的项目推向世界！ 再说一次，你的第一个个人编码项目的目的可能不应该是为你的简历构建一些令人惊叹的东西。 也就是说，我仍然认为你应该对它投入足够的爱，以至于你不会羞于在你的GitHub 个人资料上公开它，并与你认识的其他开发人员分享。 获得一些反馈，请求代码审查，您可以随时对其进行迭代，并随着您的技能提高而改进它。



原文:

While "real" hell may or may not exist (no need to get into religious beliefs here), tutorial hell is very real.

My definition of tutorial hell goes something like this:

Tutorial hell is where you write code that others are explaining to you how to write, but you don't understand how to write it yourself when given a blank slate.

At some point, it's time to take the training wheels off and build something on your own.

When is it time to build your own project?

At Boot.dev, students write their first non-tutorial project after completing the following modules:

  • Learn Python (course)
  • Learn OOP (course)
  • Build a Python CLI (project)
  • Learn Algorithms (course)
  • Learn Data Structures (course)
  • Build a Maze Solver (project)
  • Personal Project 1 (when they build their first fully custom project)

Now, keep in mind that Boot.dev students are hands-on-keyboard 100% of the time. I'm not saying you should watch 40 hours of video before building anything, but I am saying you should have a solid understanding of coding fundamentals before building your own idea. You should learn those fundamentals by writing code, but it's best if the projects and challenges you're working on are guided so that you can focus more on learning the concepts and less on figuring out what to build.

What's the goal of your first project?

Your goal here is not to build something that makes money, or even to build something that will make its way onto your resume.

The goal of this first project is simply to get you used to the process of building something from scratch. You just need to get some practice breaking down a project into smaller pieces. You need to experience that first breath of fresh air as you emerge from the depths of tutorial hell for the first time.

What are some good requirements for your first project?

  • Try to spend 20-40 hours on this project.
  • Use a programming language you're already familiar with.
  • Commit your code to Git often, and push it up to Github.
  • Create a README.md file for your project. It should explain to readers:
    • What the project is, and what it does
    • How to clone and run it
  • It's okay for (I'd even encourage) you to use third-party libraries! That said, make sure that some of the code that you write is doing something interesting. You don't want a razor-thin wrapper around a third-party library to be the only thing you've written.

But what should I build?

The whole point of a personal project is that it's personal. The idea needs to be yours. Even if the project you build is simple for now, at least it's yours.

Build something that you find interesting.

This probably isn't a project that will make its way onto your portfolio or resume, but it's still good to get into the habit of building things that are interesting and useful. Interesting and useful projects have many benefits:

  • They're more fun to work on
  • They have more interesting and difficult problems to solve
  • You (or someone else) might actually use them
  • They're more likely to grab the attention of other people
  • Attention from other people can lead to career opportunities

Use a programming language that you're already at least somewhat familiar with. Assuming you haven't been writing a ton of code outside of Boot.dev, your project should probably be a Python program that runs on the command line. That's fine!

I want to provide a few ideas to get your creative juices flowing, but please don't choose a project from this list without at least putting your own spin on it

  • A text-based adventure game in the terminal
  • A CLI tool to detect the strength of a password, or to validate it
  • Tic-Tac-Toe in a tkinter GUI
  • A tool that creates ASCII art from an image
  • A command line tool to view Reddit feeds in the terminal
  • A command line tool that parses a CSV file of pokemon data and lets you search for pokemon by name, type, etc.

But, but, but... I don't know how to build "X"!

Your job as a developer is to constantly build stuff that you've never built before. Don't shy away from an idea because you're not sure where to start. Google it. Ask ChatGPT. Ask the Discord community. You might need to put in an hour of research before starting to decide if your idea is feasible within a 40-hour time frame, but that's fine!

Assuming that you'll be doing something you've never done before! You have some fundamentals under your belt. Now it's time to piece it all together and research as you go.

You'll need help

You're going to get stuck. If you don't get stuck, you probably chose an idea that's way too easy.

The trick is developing a skillset for getting unstuck. This is a skill that will serve you well (and is absolutely necessary) in your career as a developer.

Get used to Googling

Start with Google. Do some research on your idea. See what others have done when building similar projects. See if there are any libraries you can install to make your life easier.

Use ChatGPT

Talking through your issues with ChatGPT (rubber duck debugging) can be a great way to get unstuck.

Don't have ChatGPT write your code for you! You won't learn that way, and you'll be dreadfully slow if you get stuck in that habit. Instead, use ChatGPT to help you think through your problems, and ask it to try to find bugs in your code or explain code snippets you don't understand.

Keep in mind that ChatGPT can be confidently wrong, so don't take its word as law. Use it to get back on track, but verify the information it feeds you with other sources.

Use Discord

Ask questions in the Boot.dev Discord server, especially if you're a current student (but you're welcome either way)! As with all question-asking, provide as much context as you can so that when your fellow students and mentors help they can efficiently provide you with the information you need.

Publish and share

Get your project out into the world! Again, the purpose of your first-ever personal coding project probably shouldn't be to build something amazing for your resume. That said, I still think you should put enough love into it that you're not ashamed to make it public on your GitHub profile and share it with other devs you know. Get some feedback, ask for code reviews, and you can always iterate on it later and improve it as your skills improve.

Items.Title: NestJS 与 Ditsmod：注入范围 | NestJS vs. Ditsmod: injection scopes
Items.Description:
发布于 7 月 5 日

良好的应用程序模块化与依赖注入 (DI) 创建的注入器树层次结构密切相关。 本文使用NestJS v10.0和Ditsmod v2.38进行比较。 我是Ditsmod的作者。

在 NestJS 中，没有显式定义的 DI 注入器层次结构级别，但存在隐式引用此类层次结构的范围：

  • 实例生命周期与应用程序生命周期直接相关。 一旦应用程序启动，所有单例提供程序都已实例化。 默认情况下使用单例范围。
  • 请求处理完成后，实例将被垃圾回收。
  • 每个注入临时提供者的消费者都将收到一个新的专用实例。

看来NestJS（v10.0）尚不具备在模块级别实例化提供程序的能力。 这会导致应用程序的模块化程度恶化：

应用级别。 提供程序在应用程序的生命周期中仅实例化一次（这相当于 NestJS 中的DEFAULT范围，但在 Ditsmod 中，它在第一个请求时实例化，而在 NestJS 中，实例在应用程序启动时实例化）；
  • 模块级别。 为每个模块创建一次提供程序实例；
  • 路线级别。 为每个路由创建一次提供者实例；
  • 为每个 HTTP 请求创建一次提供程序实例（这相当于 NestJS 中的REQUEST范围）。

    • 此外，在每个级别，Ditsmod 都能够每次创建特定提供程序的新实例，而无需使用注入器缓存（事实上，这相当于 NestJS 中的TRANSIENT作用域）。

    默认情况下，NestJS 将控制器实例化为单例，因此如果将这些变量分配给特定的 HTTP 请求，则开发人员无法创建类级变量。 这一功能一方面可以让应用程序的性能提高几个百分点，但另一方面，如果开发人员在控制器中为特定的 HTTP 请求。

    此外，如果具有默认作用域的控制器依赖于请求作用域的服务，那么这样的控制器也会自动（没有警告）变为请求作用域。 这样，开发人员就根本不能依赖控制器中的变量，因为并不清楚控制器最终将具有什么范围。 还有一件事：如果服务是瞬态范围的，那么控制器中不会发生类似的范围更改，这会给 NestJS 架构带来额外的不一致。

    在 NestJS v9.0 中，可以创建所谓的持久提供程序，以便拥有请求范围的服务，同时不必为每个请求重新创建依赖关系树。 根据我的测试来看，此类服务的运行速度几乎与常规请求范围的服务一样慢，但 NestJS 应用程序架构的另一个复杂之处已经增加。

    在 Ditsmod 中，每次为每个 HTTP 请求创建一个控制器实例。 无论如何，该控制器的性能与默认范围的 NestJS + Fastify 大致相同。 在任何情况下，控制器或服务的范围都不会自动更改，因此，如果应用程序级别的服务请求请求范围的服务，Ditsmod 会抛出错误，指出找不到请求范围的服务（层次结构中较高的注入器不会这样做）看不到它的子注入器）。

    结论

    如果您喜欢 NestJS，那么您很可能会更喜欢 Ditsmod。

    Костя Третяк

    Posted on Jul 5

    Good application modularity is closely related to the injector tree hierarchy that Dependency Injection (DI) creates. DI injectors are sometimes referred to as DI containers, but since NestJS and Ditsmod have taken many concepts from Angular, this post will use the term "injectors" (since it does in Angular).

    In this post, NestJS v10.0 and Ditsmod v2.38 are used for comparison. I am the author of Ditsmod.

    Scopes in NestJS

    In NestJS there are no explicitly defined levels of the DI injector hierarchy, but there are scopes that implicitly refer to such a hierarchy:

    • DEFAULT - A single instance of the provider is shared across the entire application. The instance lifetime is tied directly to the application lifecycle. Once the application has bootstrapped, all singleton providers have been instantiated. Singleton scope is used by default.
    • REQUEST - A new instance of the provider is created exclusively for each incoming request. The instance is garbage-collected after the request has completed processing.
    • TRANSIENT - Transient providers are not shared across consumers. Each consumer that injects a transient provider will receive a new, dedicated instance.

    It seems that NestJS (v10.0) does not yet have the ability to instantiate providers at the module level. This leads to deterioration of the modularity of applications:

    DI injector hierarchy in Ditsmod

    Ditsmod has 4 static levels of DI injector hierarchy:

    1. Application level. Providers are instantiated only once during the application's life cycle (this is the equivalent of the DEFAULT scope in NestJS, but in Ditsmod this is instantiated on the first request, while in NestJS the instance is instantiated at application startup);
    2. Module level. The provider instance is created once for each module;
    3. Route level. The provider instance is created once for each route;
    4. HTTP request level. A provider instance is created once for each HTTP request (this is the equivalent of the REQUEST scope in NestJS).

    In addition, at each of these levels, Ditsmod has the ability to create each time a new instance of a specific provider without using the injector cache (in fact, this is the equivalent of the TRANSIENT scope in NestJS).

    Features of controllers in NestJS

    By default, NestJS instantiates a controller as a singleton, so a developer cannot create class-level variables if those variables are assigned to a specific HTTP request. This feature, on the one hand, allows you to increase the performance of the application by several percent, but on the other hand, it increases the probability of "shooting yourself in the foot" if the developer in the controller creates a variable for a specific HTTP request.

    In addition, if a controller with a default scope has a dependency on a request-scoped service, then such a controller automatically (without warning) also becomes request-scoped. In this way, the developer cannot rely on variables in the controller at all, because it is not clear what scope the controller will have as a result. And one more thing: if the service is transient-scoped, then a similar scope change will not occur in the controller, this introduces additional inconsistency into the NestJS architecture.

    In NestJS v9.0, it became possible to create so-called durable providers in order to have request-scoped services and, at the same time, not to recreate the dependency tree for each request. Judging by my tests, such services work almost as slowly as regular request-scoped services, but another complication of the NestJS application architecture has been added.

    Features of controllers in Ditsmod

    In Ditsmod, a controller instance is created every time for every HTTP request. Regardless, performance with this controller is about the same as NestJS + Fastify with the default scope. In no case does the scope of the controller or service automatically change, so if a service at the application level requests a request-scoped service, Ditsmod throws an error that the request-scoped service is not found (the injector higher in the hierarchy does not see its child injectors).

    Conclusion

    If you like NestJS, chances are you'll like Ditsmod more.

Items.Title: 使用 Bundler 确保您的 Ruby 应用程序安全 | Keep Your Ruby App Secure with Bundler
Items.Description:
本文介绍了如何使用bundler功能来保护 Ruby 应用程序的安全。 在当今时代，我们必须越来越小心软件供应链的安全。

我们将向您展示如何通过依赖 Gemfile 和bundler来管理项目的依赖项来开始这一旅程。

在本文结束时，您将更好地了解bundler audit和bundler outdated工作原理。 两者都可以帮助您监控项目依赖关系树的安全状态。

让我们深入了解吧！

另一方面，Bundler 是一个依赖管理器。 它由 Carl Lerche 于 2009 年首次发布。

它使用 Gemfile 来定义项目所依赖的库和版本，然后以正确的顺序安装和加载这些库。 供应链安全

例如，在 2013 年、2018 年和 2020 年，出现了多个与 RubyGems 直接相关的安全问题。 每次，宝石都会受到损害，可能会将数千个项目和产品暴露给恶意行为者。

如今，由于2020年的SolarWinds黑客事件、2018年的EventStream事件以及2017年的Equifax、马士基、默克和联邦快递等重大事件，软件供应链的安全性变得更加引人注目。

让我们看看 Bundler 如何帮助我们避免一些安全问题。

源验证：确保 gems 是从特定的、可信的源安装的。
  • 通过依赖关系图，我们可以看到每个 gem 的依赖关系的完整列表，帮助我们识别潜在的安全风险。
  • 由于它与项目打包在一起，因此可以确保在不同环境中使用相同版本的 gem。
  • 漏洞检测：由于与 Ruby Advisory Database (RDB) 的集成，Bundler 可以检测 gems 中的已知安全漏洞。
  • 审核命令可让您列出与项目所依赖的 gem 相关的已知安全漏洞。
  • 校验和验证： Bundler 在安装之前验证每个 gem 的校验和。
  • 但是，唉，签署 gem 很复杂，需要很多步骤。 因此，并非所有宝石都被签署，从而使许多项目面临风险。

    然而，与此同时，我们可以特别依赖两个功能来确保项目不依赖太旧的包或已被确定为存在安全风险的包。

    这些功能对于bundler audit和bundler outdated 。 我们首先看一下bundler audit 。

    关于 Bundler 审计

    它是 Ruby 依赖管理器 Bundler 的插件，包含在 Bundler 1.10 及更高版本中。

    然后，它将这些信息与 Ruby gems 中的已知漏洞数据库（由 Rubysec 维护）进行比较。

    如果发现漏洞， bundle audit将为您提供有关该漏洞的信息，包括其严重性级别以及受影响的 gem 版本。 它还会建议您可以采取哪些措施来解决该漏洞，例如升级到 gem 的修补版本或将其完全删除。

    通过定期运行bundle audit作为开发工作流程的一部分，您可以及时了解可能影响项目的任何漏洞，并采取主动措施来解决这些漏洞。

    正如你所看到的，它输出了我们正在测试的项目所使用的包的名称和版本，其中包含一个中等级别的安全问题。 它还输出安全通报的 URL 和可能的解决方案。

    使 Bundler-audit 的数据库保持最新

    由于审核基本上是将项目中使用的 gem 列表与安全咨询数据库本地副本中的已知安全问题列表进行比较，因此您需要使该数据库保持最新。

    为此，您只需运行bundle audit update命令。 这将获取最新版本的数据库。 您只需再次运行审核即可检查是否有任何新的安全风险。

    集成到 CI 管道中

    与旨在保证项目安全的其他工具一样，最好确保定期自动运行审核（如果不是在每次提交并推送到 Git 存储库之后）。

    如果Gemfile.lock发生更改，您可以依靠 git post commit hook 在本地运行审核。 这是一个例子：

     # .git/hooks/post-commit if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 | grep -q Gemfile.lock; then echo "Gemfile.lock has changed, running audit..." bundle audit update && bundle audit fi

    这有点激进，但确实有效。 您可以在 CI 管道中依赖相同的方法。 由于bundle audit命令将返回非零退出状态，因此 CI 管道将认为它已失败。 不幸的是，我们没有现成的适当的审计报告。 相反，您必须将bundle audit命令的输出定向到文件并将其保存为 CI 管道构建的工件。

    以下是将命令的输出定向到文件的方法：

     $> bundle audit update && bundle audit > /tmp/bundle-audit-report.txt

    由于每个 CI 服务处理工件的方式不同，因此请检查相关文档以了解如何执行此操作。

    您的团队应该放置 git hooks 或 CI 任务等工具以及相关集成，以使任何问题可见。

    考虑到所有因素，我想说这并不是太麻烦。 可能需要几个小时才能开始，并将基本信息作为您最喜欢的 git 托管解决方案中的评论或 Slack 频道中的通知吐出。

    然而， bundle audit只是您可以做的事情的一部分。 它仅告诉您是否存在与项目依赖的 gem 相关的安全问题。 它不会告诉您宝石是否已过时。 为了解决这个问题，可以使用bundler outdated命令。

    红宝石宝石捆绑器已过时

    安全风险审核非常重要，但列出过时的 gem 也是一个大问题。 更新依赖项的时间越长，代码损坏的风险就越高。

    您应该以小规模迭代为目标，频繁部署，以限制每次部署中包含的更改量。 同样，您应该致力于在更新或尽可能接近更新时立即更新您使用的任何 gem。

    就像bundler audit一样， bundler outdated使用起来非常简单。 只需在 Ruby 项目的根目录中调用它即可。 它将Gemfile.lock内容与其中列出的当前 gem 版本进行比较。

     $> bundle outdated Fetching gem metadata from https://rubygems.org/......... Resolving dependencies...... Gem Current Latest Requested Groups addressable 2.8.1 2.8.4 capybara 3.38.0 3.39.0 >= 0 test devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0 default $>

    正如您所看到的，该项目中的三个 gem 已经过时了。 让我们看看应该如何阅读这张表：

    • 第一列包含宝石的名称。
    • 第二列显示当前安装的版本。
    • 第三列显示可用的最新版本。
    • 第四列显示请求版本（来自 Gemfile）。
    • 第五列显示 gem 所属的组（在 Gemfile 中）。

    在这里，我们可以看到我们不必担心可addressable和devise ，因为版本的差异在于补丁版本（版本号的第三部分）内。 尽管如此，这里要做的合乎逻辑的事情是尽快更新所有三个。

    在 CI 管道中的使用

    由于bundle outdated将为过时的 gem 返回非零退出状态，因此它所基于的 CI 任务被视为失败。 因此，您可以在 CI 管道中拥有一个简单而直接的标志，以防出现过时的依赖项。

    然而，您可能希望依靠一对标志来改进对bundle outdated的使用。 该命令可以过滤其输出，仅列出具有待处理补丁级别更新的 gem：

     $> bundle outdated --filter-patch ... Gem Current Latest Requested Groups addressable 2.8.1 2.8.4 devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0 default

    以同样的方式，您可以仅列出具有待处理的次要版本更新的 gem：

     $> bundle outdated --filter-minor Gem Current Latest Requested Groups capybara 3.38.0 3.39.0 >= 0 test

    以同样的方式，您可以仅列出具有待定主要版本更新的 gem：

     $> bundle outdated --filter-major Gem Current Latest Requested Groups sidekiq-scheduler 4.0.3 5.0.1 >= 0 default

    例如，这三个标志可让您轻松定制 CI 任务，仅警告您补丁级更新可用，但不会阻止构建（或相反）。

    一个免费选项允许您仅列出默认组中过时的宝石：

     $> bundle outdated --group default ... Gem Current Latest Requested Groups devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0 default faker 3.1.1 3.2.0 >= 0 default

    例如，由于测试和开发组中的 gem 不会影响生产版本，因此它们可能不被视为保持最新状态的优先事项。 因此，如果默认组中的 gem 已过时，您只能使用该标志来发出警告或阻止 CI 管道。

    包起来

    维护 Ruby 应用程序的安全不仅仅是一项操作的结果。 相反，它是多种行动共同作用的累积效应。 正如我们在这篇文章中介绍的，Bundler 为我们提供了两个命令，可以帮助您提高应用程序的安全性：

    • 它是对bundle audit补充，如果您持续关注它，就可以避免更新捆绑包太大。

    这两个命令易于使用，并且可以与本地反馈循环和 CI 管道集成。

    快乐编码！

    PS If you'd like to read Ruby Magic posts as soon as they get off the press, subscribe to our Ruby Magic newsletter and never miss a single post ! PS 如果您想在 Ruby Magic 帖子发布后立即阅读，请订阅我们的 Ruby Magic 时事通讯，不错过任何一篇帖子！



    • 原文:

    This article covers the use of bundler features to secure Ruby applications. In this day and age, we have to be more and more careful about software supply chain security.

    We'll show you how to start this journey by relying on a Gemfile and bundler to manage your project's dependencies.

    By the end of the post, you will better understand how bundler audit and bundler outdated work. Both can help you monitor the security state of your project's dependency tree.

    Let's dive in!

    An Introduction to Bundler for Ruby

    The history of Bundler is linked to RubyGems. RubyGems, first released in 2004 by Chad Fowler, is a package manager that makes it possible to distribute and manage Ruby libraries, applications, and their dependencies.

    Bundler, on the other hand, is a dependency manager. It was first released in 2009 by Carl Lerche.

    Bundler helps developers manage dependencies between different Ruby libraries and applications. It uses a Gemfile to define the libraries and versions that a project depends on, then installs and loads those libraries in the correct order. Bundler provides a simple way to manage dependencies and ensure that all the required libraries are installed and loaded correctly.

    Supply Chain Security

    RubyGems and Bundler can only solve some security issues. For example, in 2013, 2018, and 2020, several security issues emerged directly related to RubyGems. Each time, gems were compromised, potentially exposing thousands of projects and products to malicious actors.

    Nowadays, the security of the software supply chain has become more high profile, due to major incidents like 2020's SolarWinds hack, EventStream in 2018, and Equifax, Maersk, Merck, and FedEx in 2017.

    Let's see how Bundler can help us avoid some security issues.

    Bundler's Security-Related Features for Ruby

    Bundler comes with several features that allow us to secure a Ruby project:

    • Source validation: ensures gems are installed from a specific, trusted source.
    • With a dependency graph, we can see a whole list of dependencies for each gem, helping us to identify potential security risks.
    • A Gemfile.lock file records the specific versions of each gem used in the project. As it's packaged with the project, it ensures that the same versions of gems are used across different environments.
    • Vulnerability detection: Thanks to an integration with the Ruby Advisory Database (RDB), Bundler can detect known security vulnerabilities in gems.
    • An audit command lets you list known security vulnerabilities related to gems a project depends on.
    • Checksum verification: Bundler verifies the checksum of each gem before installing it.
    • Gem signing and signature verification: Gems can be signed cryptographically by a developer.

    But, alas, signing gems is complicated and requires a lot of steps. As a consequence, not all gems are signed, thus putting many projects at risk.

    Yet, in the meantime, we can rely on two features in particular to ensure that a project doesn't rely on packages that are too old or that have been identified as carrying a security risk.

    These features are bundler audit and bundler outdated. Let's look at bundler audit first.

    About Bundler-audit

    bundle audit is a command-line tool that helps you identify security vulnerabilities in the Ruby libraries that your project depends on. It is a plugin for the Ruby dependency manager Bundler and is included with Bundler version 1.10 and above.

    bundle audit works by analyzing your Gemfile.lock file, which lists all the dependencies for your project and their versions. It then compares this information with a database of known vulnerabilities in Ruby gems (maintained by Rubysec).

    If a vulnerability is found, bundle audit will provide you with information about the vulnerability, including its severity level and which gem versions have been affected. It will also suggest actions you can take to address the vulnerability, such as upgrading to a patched version of the gem or removing it entirely.

    bundle audit is a useful tool for ensuring the security of your Ruby projects, especially if you are using third-party libraries or dependencies. By regularly running bundle audit as part of your development workflow, you can stay up-to-date on any vulnerabilities that may affect your project and take proactive steps to address them.

    Usage of Bundler-audit in Ruby

    The basic use of Bundler-audit is through the bundle audit command:

    $> bundle-audit Name: actionpack
Version: 3.2.10
Advisory: OSVDB-91452
Criticality: Medium
URL: http://www.osvdb.org/show/osvdb/91452
Title: XSS vulnerability in sanitize_css in Action Pack
Solution: upgrade to ~> 2.3.18, ~> 3.1.12, >= 3.2.13 $>

    As you can see, it outputs the name and version of a package used by the project we are testing, which contains a medium-level security issue. It also outputs the URL of the security advisory and a possible solution.

    Keeping Bundler-audit's Database Up to Date

    As the audit is basically comparing a list of gems used in the project with a list of known security issues in a local copy of the security advisory database, you need to keep that database up to date.

    To do so, you just need to run the bundle audit update command. This will fetch the latest version of the database. You should only have to run the audit again to check for any new security risks.

    Integration In a CI Pipeline

    As with other tools aimed at keeping your project safe, it's best to ensure an audit runs automatically on a regular basis (if not after every commit and push to the Git repository).

    You can rely on a git post commit hook to run the audit locally if the Gemfile.lock changes. Here is an example:

    # .git/hooks/post-commit
if git diff --name-only HEAD~1 | grep -q Gemfile.lock; then echo "Gemfile.lock has changed, running audit..." bundle audit update && bundle audit
fi

    This is a bit radical, but it works. You can rely on the same approach within the CI pipeline. As the bundle audit command will return a non-zero exit status, the CI pipeline will consider it to have failed. Unfortunately, we don't have a proper audit report out of the box. Instead, you have to direct the output of the bundle audit command towards a file and save it as an artifact of the build for the CI pipeline.

    Here is how to direct the command's output to a file:

    $> bundle audit update && bundle audit > /tmp/bundle-audit-report.txt

    As each CI service handles artifacts differently, check the relevant documentation to see how to do this.

    Bundler-audit in Summary

    bundle audit is not a complete solution, yet it still lets your team know if your project relies on unsafe gems (without costing you an arm and a leg). Your team should put tools such as git hooks or CI tasks in place, and relevant integrations to make any issues visible.

    All things considered, I'd say it's not too much trouble. It probably requires a few hours to get started and have the basic information spit out as a comment in your favorite git hosting solution, or as a notice in a Slack channel.

    Yet bundle audit is only one part of what you can do. It only tells you if there is a security issue related to a gem relied on by a project. It does not tell you if a gem is outdated. To handle that, bundler outdated is the command to use.

    Bundler Outdated for Ruby Gems

    Auditing for security risks is very important, yet listing outdated gems is also a big issue. The longer you wait to update a dependency, the higher the risk that your code breaks.

    You should aim to work in small iterations, deployed frequently, to limit the amount of change contained in each deployment. Equally, you should aim to update any gem you use as soon as it's updated, or as close to that as possible.

    Usage of Bundler Outdated

    Just like bundler audit, bundler outdated is very simple to use. Just call it at the root of your Ruby project. It will compare the Gemfile.lock content to the current gem releases listed in it.

    $> bundle outdated
Fetching gem metadata from https://rubygems.org/.........
Resolving dependencies...... Gem Current Latest Requested Groups
addressable 2.8.1 2.8.4
capybara 3.38.0 3.39.0 >= 0 test
devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0 default $>

    As you can see, three gems are outdated in this project. Let's see how we should read this table:

    • The first column contains the name of the gem.
    • The second column shows the currently installed version.
    • The third column shows the latest version available.
    • The fourth column shows the request version (from the Gemfile).
    • The fifth column shows the group the gem is part of (in the Gemfile).

    Here, we can see we don't have much to worry about for addressable and devise, as the difference in releases is within the patch versions (third part of the release number). The version of capybara is only behind one minor release, which implies a few more changes, but this rarely means breaking changes.

    Still, the logical thing to do here is to update all three as soon as possible.

    Usage In a CI Pipeline

    As bundle outdated will return a non-zero exit status for outdated gems, a CI task it's based on is considered failed. So you can have a simple and direct flag in your CI pipeline in case of outdated dependencies.

    Yet you might want to rely on a pair of flags to improve your use of bundle outdated. The command can filter its output to only list gems that have pending patch-level updates:

    $> bundle outdated --filter-patch
...
Gem Current Latest Requested Groups
addressable 2.8.1 2.8.4
devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0    default

    In the same manner, you can list only gems with pending minor version updates:

    $> bundle outdated --filter-minor
Gem Current Latest Requested Groups
capybara 3.38.0 3.39.0 >= 0 test

    And in the same way, you can list only gems with pending major release updates:

    $> bundle outdated --filter-major
Gem Current Latest Requested Groups
sidekiq-scheduler 4.0.3 5.0.1 >= 0       default

    Those three flags let you easily tailor a CI task to only warn you that a patch-level update is available but not block the build (or the reverse), for example.

    A complimentary option allows you to only list outdated gems within the default group:

    $> bundle outdated --group default
...
Gem Current Latest Requested Groups
devise 4.9.0 4.9.2 >= 4.6.0 default
faker 3.1.1 3.2.0 >= 0               default

    As gems within test and development groups don't impact the production release, they could be considered less of a priority to keep up to date, for example. Thus you can use the flag only to raise a warning or block the CI pipeline if gems within the default group are outdated.

    Wrapping Up

    Maintaining the security of your Ruby application is not just the result of one action. Rather, it's a cumulative effect of several actions put together. As we've covered in this post, Bundler provides us with two commands that can help you to improve your application's security:

    • bundle audit quickly lets you know if any gem you add or use in your project could pose a security threat and what to do about it.
    • bundle outdated allows you to flag gems that should be updated. It complements bundle audit, and if you keep on top of it, you avoid having an update bundle that's too large.

    These two commands are easy to use and integrate with local feedback loops and CI pipelines.

    Happy coding!

    P.S. If you'd like to read Ruby Magic posts as soon as they get off the press, subscribe to our Ruby Magic newsletter and never miss a single post!

Items.Title: 嗨，我需要星星 | Hi I need Stars
Items.Description:
一旦被暂停，桑蒂玛斯将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，桑蒂玛斯将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，santimars 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 santimars 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 santimars：

使 santimars 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

不加标记的 santimars 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, santimars will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, santimars will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by santimars will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If santimars is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag santimars:

Make all posts by santimars less visible

santimars consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging santimars will restore default visibility to their posts.

Items.Title: 通过制作 RPG 来学习戈多 4 — 第 17 部分：基本 NPC 和任务🤠 | Let’s Learn Godot 4 by Making an RPG — Part 17: Basic NPC & Quest🤠
Items.Description:
现在我们已经完成了游戏的大部分设置，我们可以继续创建一个带有任务的 NPC。 请注意，这个任务系统不会是一个带有任务链的动态系统。 不，不幸的是，这个任务系统只包含一个带有对话框和状态树的简单收集任务。 我将其保留为这个简单的系统，因为对话、任务甚至库存系统都非常复杂，因此我将制作一个单独的教程系列，单独关注这些高级概念！

您将在本部分中学到什么：

  • 如何使用游戏状态。
  • 如何添加对话树。
  • 进一步练习弹出窗口和动画。
  • 如何使用 InputEventKey 对象。
  • 如何将组添加到节点。
  • 如何使用 RayCast2D 节点。

如果您对这些系统如何工作感到好奇，您可以找到一些书面资源，让您了解如何在下面实现这一点。

资源：

让我们创建 NPC 场景。 在您的项目中，创建一个新场景，将 CharacterBody2D 节点作为场景根，将 AnimatedSprite2D 和 CollisionBody2D 节点作为其子节点。 将碰撞节点的碰撞形状设为 RectangleShape。 将此场景保存在“场景”文件夹下。

让我们向 AnimatedSprite2D 节点添加两个新动画：idle_down 和 talk_down。 您可以在 Assets > Mobs > Coyote 下找到我用于该节点的动画 spritesheet。

空闲状态：

谈话：

目前，我们的 NPC 将锚定在一处，他们不会攻击我们的敌人。 如果您想让我向您展示如何让它们在地图上漫游并攻击敌人，请告诉我，反之亦然。 将 FPS 和 Looping 保留为其默认值。

将脚本附加到 NPC 场景并将其保存在 Scripts 文件夹下。

我们还想向此节点添加一个组，以便我们的玩家在与其交互时可以检查特殊的“NPC”组。 您可以将节点添加到任意数量的组中。 我们还必须向播放器添加一个 RayCast2D 节点，以便我们可以检查其“目标”，如果该目标是 NPC 组的一部分，我们将启动与 NPC 对话的功能。

要将组添加到 NPC 节点，请选择其根节点，然后在通常连接信号的检查器面板中，单击组的属性。 然后添加一个名为“NPC”的新组。

弹出对话框和播放器设置

现在，在您的 Player 场景中，我们需要创建另一个弹出节点，当玩家与 NPC 交互时该节点将可见。 添加一个新的 Popup 节点并将其重命名为“DialogPopup”。

将其大小更改为 (x: 300, y: 80)，将其位置更改为 (x: 10: y: 90)。

在此 DialogPopup 节点中，我们添加一个 ColorRect，并将三个 Label 节点作为其子节点。 将其重命名如下：

选择您的对话框节点并将其颜色属性更改为#581929。 更改其大小（x：500，y：80）及其anchor_preset（全矩形）。

选择 NPC Label 节点并将其文本更改为“Name”。 更改其大小（x：290，y：26）； 位置（x：5，y：2）。

现在将其字体更改为“Scrondinger”，并将字体大小更改为 10。我们还想更改其字体颜色，因此在“颜色”>“字体颜色”下，将颜色更改为#B26245。

选择您的消息标签节点并将其文本更改为“此处文本...”。 更改其大小（x：290，y：30）； 位置（x：5，y：15）。 还将其字体更改为“Scrondinger”，并将字体大小更改为 10。AutoWrap 属性也应设置为“Word”。

选择您的响应标签节点并将其文本更改为“Answer”。 更改其大小（x：290，y：16）； 位置（x：5，y：60）； 水平和垂直对齐（中心）。 另外，将其字体更改为“Scrondinger”，并将字体大小更改为 10。在“颜色”>“字体颜色”下，将颜色更改为#D6c376。

将 DialogPopups 可见性更改为隐藏，并在 UI 层之前添加 RayCast2D 节点。

在您的玩家脚本中，当我们将光线投射节点的方向设置为与角色的方向相同时，我们必须执行与在敌人脚本中相同的操作。 在 _physical_process() 函数中，让我们转动 RayCast2D 节点以跟随玩家的移动方向。

 ### Player.gd #older code func _physics_process(delta): # Get player input (left, right, up/down) var direction: Vector2 direction.x = Input.get_action_strength("ui_right") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_left") direction.y = Input.get_action_strength("ui_down") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_up") # If input is digital, normalize it for diagonal movement if abs(direction.x) == 1 and abs(direction.y) == 1: direction = direction.normalized() #sprinting if Input.is_action_pressed("ui_sprint"): while stamina >= 25: speed = 100 stamina = stamina - 5 stamina_updated.emit(stamina, max_stamina) elif Input.is_action_just_released("ui_sprint"): speed = 50 # Apply movement var movement = speed * direction * delta if !is_attacking: # moves our player around, whilst enforcing collisions so that they come to a stop when colliding into other object. move_and_collide(movement) #plays animations only if the player is not attacking player_animations(direction) # Turn RayCast2D toward movement direction if direction != Vector2.ZERO: $RayCast2D.target_position = direction.normalized() * 50

该光线投射将击中分配有碰撞体的任何节点。 我们希望它击中我们的 NPC，如果击中了，并且我们按下交互按钮，它将启动对话框弹出窗口并运行对话框树。 为此，我们需要首先添加一个新的输入操作，按下该操作即可与 NPC 进行交互。

在项目设置的输入操作菜单中，添加一个名为“ui_interact”的新输入并为其分配一个键。 我将键盘上的 TAB 键指定给此操作。

现在，在 input() 函数中，我们将对其进行扩展，为 ui_interact 操作添加输入事件。 如果我们的玩家按下此按钮，我们的光线投射将捕获它所击中的碰撞体，如果这些碰撞体之一是“NPC”组的一部分，它将启动 NPC 对话框。 我们在敌人场景中做了类似的事情。

 ### Player.gd #older code #plays attacking animation func _input(event): #input event for our attacking, ie our shooting if event.is_action_pressed("ui_attack"): #checks the current time as the amount of time passed in milliseconds since the engine started var now = Time.get_ticks_msec() #check if player can shoot if the reload time has passed and we have ammo if now >= bullet_fired_time and ammo_pickup > 0: #shooting anim is_attacking = true var animation = "attack_" + returned_direction(new_direction) $AnimatedSprite2D.play(animation) #bullet fired time time to current time bullet_fired_time = now + bullet_reload_time #reduce and signal ammo change ammo_pickup = ammo_pickup - 1 ammo_pickups_updated.emit(ammo_pickup) #using health consumables elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_health"): if health > 0 && health_pickup > 0: health_pickup = health_pickup - 1 health = min(health + 50, max_health) health_updated.emit(health, max_health) health_pickups_updated.emit(health_pickup) #using stamina consumables elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_stamina"): if stamina > 0 && stamina_pickup > 0: stamina_pickup = stamina_pickup - 1 stamina = min(stamina + 50, max_stamina) stamina_updated.emit(stamina, max_stamina) stamina_pickups_updated.emit(stamina_pickup) #interact with world elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_interact"): var target = $RayCast2D.get_collider() if target != null: if target.is_in_group("NPC"): # Talk to NPC #todo: add dialog function to npc return

接下来，我们希望 NPC 脚本更新 Player 场景标签（npc、消息和响应）中的 DialogPopup 节点。 我们还希望它能够处理玩家输入的对话框响应，因此如果玩家说“A”或“B”表示是/否，对话框弹出窗口将根据对话框树更新消息值。 当我们稍后设置对话框树时，我们将对此进行更多讨论。

将新脚本附加到 DialogPopup 节点并将其保存在 GUI 文件夹下。

有时，您希望类的成员变量不仅仅保存数据并在其值发生变化时执行一些验证或计算。 还可能需要以某种方式封装其访问。 为此，GDScript 提供了一种特殊的语法，可以在变量声明后使用set 和 get关键字来定义属性。 然后，您可以定义一个代码块，该代码块将在访问或分配变量时执行。

在此脚本的顶部，我们需要声明一些变量。 我们将使用 set/get 属性定义这些变量，这将允许我们根据从 NPC 脚本获取的数据设置标签值。 我们还需要为 NPC 引用定义一个变量。

 ####DialogPopup.gd extends Popup #gets the values of our npc from our NPC scene and sets it in the label values var nickname : set = npc_name_set var message: set = message_set var response: set = response_set #reference to NPC var npc

接下来，我们需要创建三个函数来设置 set/get 变量的值。 这将捕获从 NPC 传递的值并将该值分配给我们的标签。

 ####DialogPopup.gd #older code #sets the npc name with the value received from NPC func npc_name_set(new_value): npc_name = new_value $Dialog/NPC.text = new_value #sets the message with the value received from NPC func message_set(new_value): message = new_value $Dialog/Message.text = new_value #sets the response with the value received from NPC func response_set(new_value): response = new_value $Dialog/Response.text = new_value

在继续此脚本之前，让我们向消息节点添加一个动画，使其具有打字机效果。 这是RPG类型游戏中的经典功能，因此我们也将实现这一点。 在 Godot 4 中，我们可以通过更改动画播放器中文本的可见比例来实现此目的。 这将使我们的文本可见性慢慢地从不可见转变为可见。

在 Player 场景的 AnimationPlayer 节点中，添加一个名为“打字机”的新动画。

将新的属性轨道添加到您的动画并将其分配给您的消息节点。

我们要更改的属性是对话框文本的可见比例。

此可见比率将使对话比率从 0 到 1 可见。在visible_ratio 轨道中，添加两个新关键点，一个位于关键帧 0，另一个位于关键帧 1。

将关键帧 0 的值更改为 0，将关键帧 1 的值更改为 1。

现在，如果您播放动画，您的打字机效果应该可以工作！

回到 DialogPopup 代码，让我们创建两个由 NPC 脚本调用的函数。 第一个函数应该暂停游戏并打开弹出对话框并播放打字机动画。 我们可以使用其内置的popup()函数打开弹出窗口。 另一个函数应该关闭对话框并取消暂停游戏。 我们可以使用其内置的hide()函数关闭弹出窗口。

 ####DialogPopup.gd #older code #opens the dialog func open(): get_tree().paused = true $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter") popup() #closes the dialog func close(): get_tree().paused = false hide()

如果此弹出窗口被隐藏并且消息文本尚未完成，则此弹出窗口不应接收输入。 因此，在_ready ()函数中，我们必须调用set_process_input()函数来禁用输入处理。 这将禁用我们的 Player 脚本中的输入函数和输入单例处理任何输入。

 ####DialogPopup.gd #older code #no input on hidden func _ready(): set_process_input(false) #opens the dialog func open(): get_tree().paused = true $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter") popup() #closes the dialog func close(): set_process_input(false) get_tree().paused = false hide()

我们只希望玩家能够在“打字机”动画完成消息文本动画后插入输入。 因此，我们可以将AnimationPlayer节点的animation_finished()信号连接到我们的DialogPopup脚本。


 ####DialogPopup.gd #older code #receive input after animation plays func _on_animation_player_animation_finished(anim_name): set_process_input(true)

最后，我们可以编写代码来接受玩家的输入以响应我们的对话框选项。 我们不必为此创建唯一的输入操作，因为我们只需使用InputEventKey对象即可。 该对象存储键盘上的按键操作。 因此，如果我们按“A”或“B”，该对象将捕获这些键作为输入并触发对话树来响应这些输入。

这是一个直观的表示，可以帮助您了解我们想要实现的目标：


 ####DialogPopup.gd #older code func _input(event): if event is InputEventKey: if event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_A: #todo: add dialog function to npc return elif event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_B: #todo: add dialog function to npc return

一旦我们的 NPC 脚本中有了对话函数，我们就会回到这个函数。 我们现在要开始处理这个问题，所以让我们打开 NPC 脚本并定义一些变量来存储我们的任务和对话状态。 我们将创建一个枚举来保存任务的状态——即任务未开始、开始和完成时的状态。 然后我们将实例化该枚举以将其初始状态设置为NOT_STARTED 。 然后我们需要将对话框的状态存储为整数。

我们将对话框状态定义为一个整数，以便我们可以在整个对话框树中递增它，然后我们可以根据匹配语句中的数值选择对话框。 它相当于许多其他语言中的 switch 语句，其工作方式是将表达式与模式进行比较，如果该模式匹配，则将执行相应的块。 之后，在 match 语句下面继续执行。

我们的对话和任务状态将在此匹配大小写模式中执行：

 match (quest_status): NOT_STARTED: match (dialog_state): 0: //dialog message //increase dialog state match (answer): //dialog message //increase dialog state 1: //dialog message //increase dialog state match (answer): //dialog message //increase dialog state STARTED: match (dialog_state): 0: //dialog message //increase dialog state match (answer): //dialog message //increase dialog state 1: //dialog message //increase dialog state match (answer): //dialog message //increase dialog state COMPLETED: match (dialog_state): 0: //dialog message //increase dialog state match (answer): //dialog message

让我们定义变量。

现在我们可以创建我们的dialog()函数。 该对话框树相当长，因此我建议您继续从下面复制并粘贴它。 对话树或对话树是一种游戏机制，在玩家角色与 NPC 交互时运行。 在这棵树中，玩家可以选择要说什么，并做出后续选择，直到对话结束。

在我们的对话树中，我们的 NPC 引导玩家完成寻找食谱书的任务。 我们还没有创建这本食谱书或任务物品，它将呼叫我们的 NPC 通知他们任务已完成。 如果我们还没有拿到这个任务物品，NPC会提醒我们去领取。 如果我们得到了这个任务物品，NPC会感谢我们并奖励我们，并完成任务。

I haven't seen anybody round these parts in quite a while. That reminds me, I recently lost my momma's secret recipe book, can you help me find it?" dialog_popup.response = "[A] Yes [B] No" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog 1: match response: "A": # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 2 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "That's mighty kind of you, thanks." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog "B": # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 3 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "Well, I'll be waiting like a tumbleweed 'till you come back." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog 2: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 0 quest_status = QuestStatus.STARTED # Close dialog popup dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle" $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") 3: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle" $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") QuestStatus.STARTED: match dialog_state: 0: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 1 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "Found that book yet?" if quest_complete: dialog_popup.response = "[A] Yes [B] No" else: dialog_popup.response = "[A] No" dialog_popup.open() 1: if quest_complete and response == "A": # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 2 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "Yeehaw! Now I can make cat-eye soup. Here, take this." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() else: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 3 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "I'm so hungry, please hurry..." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() 2: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 0 quest_status = QuestStatus.COMPLETED # Close dialog popup dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle" $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") # Add potion and XP to the player. player.add_pickup(Pickups.AMMO) player.update_xp(50) 3: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle" $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") QuestStatus.COMPLETED: match dialog_state: 0: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 1 # Show dialog popup dialog_popup.message = "Nice seeing you again partner!" dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() 1: # Update dialog tree state dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle" $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down")

不幸的是，我们的任务和对话与我们的 NPC 直接相关——在真实的游戏中，您的脚本中会包含 NPC、任务和对话系统。 我将就此制作一个单独的教程系列，但现在，我只想向您展示对话框树和状态的基础知识。

如果您想要多个 NPC，则必须复制 NPC 场景和脚本，以及任务物品场景和脚本 — 然后只需更新这些值即可拥有唯一的对话框和对第二个 NPC 的引用。 我将在上面的代码参考中包含另一个 NPC 的示例。

故障排除：我的对话框弹出窗口冻结了我的游戏！

如果您看到此问题，那是因为我们的 open() 函数正在暂停我们的游戏，并且它阻止我们的动画完成以及我们的玩家输入任何输入。 另外，这并不是因为我们的暂停状态首先在 open() 函数中分配 - 我已经测试过它，并且无论您在动画和弹出窗口打开之前还是之后暂停游戏都没有关系。 此代码适用于 Godot 4.02 及以下版本，但不适用于 Godot 4.03。 为了解决这个问题，我们必须对 DialogPopup 代码进行一些更改。

首先，我们需要将播放器的处理模式更改为“始终”。 这意味着即使游戏暂停，我们的玩家也能够向游戏添加输入。

如果我们现在玩游戏，就会出现问题，因为当对话框运行时，我们的玩家将能够离开 NPC，因此为了解决这个问题，我们需要禁用玩家的在Physics_process() 函数中执行的移动。 为此，我们只需在 open() 函数末尾调用它并将其处理设置为 false！ 然后在我们的 close 函数中，我们只需将其设置回 true，因为如果对话框弹出窗口被隐藏，我们希望我们的玩家再次移动。

 ####DialogPopup.gd extends Popup #gets the values of our npc from our NPC scene and sets it in the label values var npc_name : set = npc_name_set var message: set = message_set var response: set = response_set #reference to NPC var npc #sets the npc name with the value received from NPC func npc_name_set(new_value): npc_name = new_value $Dialog/NPC.text = new_value #sets the message with the value received from NPC func message_set(new_value): message = new_value $Dialog/Message.text = new_value #sets the response with the value received from NPC func response_set(new_value): response = new_value $Dialog/Response.text = new_value #no input on hidden func _ready(): set_process_input(false) #opens the dialog func open(): get_tree().paused = true popup() $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter") $"../..".set_physics_process(false) func _input(event): if event is InputEventKey: if event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_A: npc.dialog("A") elif event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_B: npc.dialog("B") #closes the dialog func close(): set_process_input(false) get_tree().paused = false hide() $"../..".set_physics_process(true) #receive input after animation plays func _on_animation_player_animation_finished(anim_name): set_process_input(true)

现在你就拥有了！ 现在你的游戏中有一个有任务的 NPC。 我们将从这里添加的其余功能将很快实现。 大部分艰苦的工作已经完成，所以现在我们只需要添加场景转换能力来在世界之间传送我们的玩家，并且我们还将在下一部分中为我们的玩家提供睡眠能力以恢复他们的生命值和耐力值！ 请记住保存您的项目，我们下一部分见。
