原文:

Now that we have most of our game set up, we can go ahead and create an NPC with a Quest. Please note that this quest system will not be a dynamic system with quest chains. No, unfortunately, this quest system only contains a simple collection quest with a dialog and state tree. I left it to be this simple system because a dialog, quest, and even inventory system is quite complex, and therefore I will make a separate tutorial series that will focus on those advanced concepts in isolation!

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN IN THIS PART:

  • How to work with game states.
  • How to add dialog trees.
  • Further practice with popups and animations.
  • How to work with the InputEventKey object.
  • How to add Groups to nodes.
  • How to work with the RayCast2D node.

If you are curious about how these systems might work, you can find some written resources that could give you an idea of how to implement this below.

Resources:

NPC Setup

Let’s create our NPC scene. In your project, create a new scene with a CharacterBody2D node as the scene root, and an AnimatedSprite2D and CollisionBody2D node as its children. Make the collision shape for your collision node to be a RectangleShape. Save this scene underneath your Scenes folder.

Let’s add two new animations to the AnimatedSprite2D node: idle_down and talk_down. You can find the animations spritesheet that I used for this node underneath Assets > Mobs > Coyote.

idle_down:

talk_down:

For now, our NPC will be anchored in one place, and they won’t attack our enemies. Let me know if you want me to show you how to make them roam around the map and attack enemies and vice versa. Leave the FPS and Looping as their default values.

Attach a script to your NPC scene and save it underneath your Scripts folder.

We also want to add a Group to this node so that our players can check for the special “NPC” group when they interact with it. Groups in Godot work like tags in other software. You can add a node to as many groups as you want. We will have to also add a RayCast2D node to our player so that we can check its “target”, and if that target is part of the NPC group, we’ll launch the function to talk to the NPC.

To add a Group to your NPC node, select its root node and in the Inspector panel where you usually connect signals, click on the Group’s property. Then add a new group called “NPC”.

Dialog Popup & Player Setup

Now, in your Player scene, we need to create another popup node that will be made visible when the player interacts with the NPC. Add a new Popup node and rename it to “DialogPopup”.

Change its size to (x: 300, y: 80) and its position to (x: 10: y: 90).

In this DialogPopup node, let’s add a ColorRect with three Label nodes as its children. Rename it as follows:

Select your Dialog node and change its Color property to #581929. Change its size (x: 500, y: 80), and its anchor_preset (Full Rect).

Select your NPC Label node and change its text to “Name”. Change its size (x: 290, y: 26); position (x: 5, y: 2).

Now change its font to “Scrondinger”, and its font size to 10. We also want to change its font color, so underneath Color > Font Color, change the color to #B26245.

Select your Message Label node and change its text to “Text here…”. Change its size (x: 290, y: 30); position (x: 5, y: 15). Also change its font to “Scrondinger”, and its font size to 10. The AutoWrap property should also be set to “Word”.

Select your Response Label node and change its text to “Answer”. Change its size (x: 290, y: 16); position (x: 5, y: 60); horizontal and vertical alignment (center). Also, change its font to “Scrondinger”, and its font size to 10. Underneath Color > Font Color, change the color to #D6c376.

Change the DialogPopups visibility to be hidden and add a RayCast2D node before your UI layer.

In your Player script, we have to do the same as we did in our Enemy script when we set the direction of our raycast node to be the same as the direction of our character. In your _physics_process() function, let’s turn the RayCast2D node to follow our player’s movement direction.

 ### Player.gd
 #older code
 func _physics_process(delta): # Get player input (left, right, up/down)
 var direction: Vector2 direction.x = Input.get_action_strength("ui_right") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_left") direction.y = Input.get_action_strength("ui_down") - Input.get_action_strength("ui_up") # If input is digital, normalize it for diagonal movement
 if abs(direction.x) == 1 and abs(direction.y) == 1: direction = direction.normalized() #sprinting  if Input.is_action_pressed("ui_sprint"): while stamina >= 25: speed = 100 stamina = stamina - 5 stamina_updated.emit(stamina, max_stamina) elif Input.is_action_just_released("ui_sprint"): speed = 50 # Apply movement
 var movement = speed * direction * delta if !is_attacking: # moves our player around, whilst enforcing collisions so that they come to a stop when colliding into other object.
 move_and_collide(movement) #plays animations only if the player is not attacking
 player_animations(direction) # Turn RayCast2D toward movement direction  if direction != Vector2.ZERO: $RayCast2D.target_position = direction.normalized() * 50

This raycast will hit any node that has a collision body assigned to it. We want it to hit our NPC, and if it does and we press our interaction button, it will launch the dialog popup and run the dialog tree. For this, we need to first add a new input action that we can press to interact with NPCs.

In your Input Actions menu in your Project Settings, add a new input called “ui_interact” and assign a key to it. I assigned the TAB key on my keyboard to this action.

Now, in our input() function, we will expand on it to add an input event for our ui_interact action. If our player presses this button, our raycast will capture the colliders it’s hitting, and if one of those colliders is part of the “NPC” group, it will launch the NPC dialog. We’ve done something similar to this in our Enemy scene.

 ### Player.gd
 #older code
 #plays attacking animation
 func _input(event): #input event for our attacking, i.e. our shooting
 if event.is_action_pressed("ui_attack"): #checks the current time as the amount of time passed in milliseconds since the engine started
 var now = Time.get_ticks_msec() #check if player can shoot if the reload time has passed and we have ammo
 if now >= bullet_fired_time and ammo_pickup > 0: #shooting anim
 is_attacking = true var animation = "attack_" + returned_direction(new_direction) $AnimatedSprite2D.play(animation) #bullet fired time time to current time
 bullet_fired_time = now + bullet_reload_time #reduce and signal ammo change
 ammo_pickup = ammo_pickup - 1 ammo_pickups_updated.emit(ammo_pickup) #using health consumables
 elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_health"): if health > 0 && health_pickup > 0: health_pickup = health_pickup - 1 health = min(health + 50, max_health) health_updated.emit(health, max_health) health_pickups_updated.emit(health_pickup) #using stamina consumables  elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_stamina"): if stamina > 0 && stamina_pickup > 0: stamina_pickup = stamina_pickup - 1 stamina = min(stamina + 50, max_stamina) stamina_updated.emit(stamina, max_stamina) stamina_pickups_updated.emit(stamina_pickup) #interact with world  elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_interact"): var target = $RayCast2D.get_collider() if target != null: if target.is_in_group("NPC"): # Talk to NPC
 #todo: add dialog function to npc
 return

Next up we want our NPC script to update the DialogPopup node in our Player scene’s labels (npc, message, and response). We also want it to handle the player’s input for the dialog’s response, so if the player says “A” or “B” for yes/no the dialog popup will update the message values according to the dialog tree. We’ll get more into this when we set up our dialog tree later on.

Attach a new script to your DialogPopup node and save it underneath your GUI folder.

Sometimes, you want a class’s member variable to do more than just hold data and perform some validation or computation whenever its value changes. It may also be desired to encapsulate its access in some way. For this, GDScript provides a special syntax to define properties using the set and get keywords after a variable declaration. Then you can define a code block that will be executed when the variable is accessed or assigned.

At the top of this script, we need to declare a few variables. We will define these variables using our set/get properties, which will allow us to set our Label values from the data that we get from our NPC script. We also need to define a variable for our NPC reference.

 ####DialogPopup.gd
 extends Popup #gets the values of our npc from our NPC scene and sets it in the label values
 var nickname : set = npc_name_set var message: set = message_set var response: set = response_set #reference to NPC
 var npc

Next, we need to create three functions to set the values of our set/get variables. This will capture the value passed from our NPC and assign the value to our Labels.

 ####DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 #sets the npc name with the value received from NPC
 func npc_name_set(new_value): npc_name = new_value $Dialog/NPC.text = new_value #sets the message with the value received from NPC
 func message_set(new_value): message = new_value $Dialog/Message.text = new_value #sets the response with the value received from NPC
 func response_set(new_value): response = new_value $Dialog/Response.text = new_value

Before we continue with this script, let’s add an animation to our Message node so that it has a typewriter effect. This is a classic feature in RPG-type games, so we will also implement this. In Godot 4, we can do this by changing our text’s visible ratio in the AnimationPlayer. This will transition our text visibility slowly from invisible to visible.

In your AnimationPlayer node in your Player scene, add a new animation called “typewriter”.

Add a new Property Track to your animation and assign it to your Message node.

The property we want to change is the visible ratio of our dialog text.

This visible ratio will make the dialog ratio visible from 0 to 1. In your visible_ratio track, add two new keys, one at keyframe 0 and the other one at keyframe 1.

Change the Value of your keyframe 0 to 0, and the Value of your keyframe 1 to 1.

Now if you play your animation, your typewriter effect should work!

Back in your DialogPopup code, let’s create two functions that will be called by our NPC script. The first function should pause the game and open up the dialog popup and play the typewriter animation. We can open up the popup using its built-in popup() function. The other function should close the dialog and unpause the game. We can close the popup using its built-in hide() function.

 ####DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 #opens the dialog
 func open(): get_tree().paused = true $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter") popup() #closes the dialog  func close(): get_tree().paused = false hide()

If this popup is hidden and if the message text has not been completed yet, this popup should not receive input. So, in the _ready() function, we must call the set_process_input() function to disable input handling. This will disable the input function and Input singleton in our Player script from processing any input.

 ####DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 #no input on hidden
 func _ready(): set_process_input(false) #opens the dialog
 func open(): get_tree().paused = true $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter") popup() #closes the dialog  func close(): set_process_input(false) get_tree().paused = false hide()

We only want the player to be able to insert inputs if our “typewriter” animation has finished animating our message text. Therefore, we can connect the AnimationPlayer node’s animation_finished() signal to our DialogPopup script.


 ####DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 #receive input after animation plays  func _on_animation_player_animation_finished(anim_name): set_process_input(true)

Finally, we can write our code that will accept the player’s input in response to our dialog options. We don’t have to create unique input actions for this, as we can just use the InputEventKey object. This object stores key presses on the keyboard. So if we press “A” or “B”, the object will capture those keys as input and trigger the dialog tree to respond to these inputs.

Here’s a visual representation to help you understand what we want to achieve:


 ####DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 func _input(event): if event is InputEventKey: if event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_A: #todo: add dialog function to npc
 return elif event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_B: #todo: add dialog function to npc
 return

NPC Dialog Tree

We’ll come back to this function once we have the dialog function in our NPC script. We’re going to start working on that now, so let’s open up our NPC script and define some variables that will store our quest and dialog states. We’ll create an enum that will hold the states of our Quest — which is when it’s not started, started, and completed. then we will instance that enum to set its initial state to be NOT_STARTED. We then need to store the state of our dialog as an integer.

We’ll define our dialog state as an integer so that we can increment it throughout our dialog tree, and then we can select our dialog based on our number value in a match statement. A match statement is used to branch the execution of a program. It’s the equivalent of the switch statement found in many other languages, and it works in the way that an expression is compared to a pattern, and if that pattern matches, the corresponding block will be executed. After that, the execution continues below the match statement.

Our dialog and quest states will be executed in this match-case pattern:

match (quest_status):
 NOT_STARTED: 
  match (dialog_state):
   0:
    //dialog message
    //increase dialog state
    match (answer):
     //dialog message
     //increase dialog state
   1:
    //dialog message
    //increase dialog state
    match (answer):
     //dialog message
     //increase dialog state
    STARTED: 
  match (dialog_state):
   0:
    //dialog message
    //increase dialog state
    match (answer):
     //dialog message
     //increase dialog state
   1:
    //dialog message
    //increase dialog state
    match (answer):
     //dialog message
     //increase dialog state
    COMPLETED: 
  match (dialog_state):
   0:
    //dialog message
    //increase dialog state
    match (answer):
     //dialog message

Let’s define our variables.

 ### NPC
 extends CharacterBody2D #quest and dialog states
 enum QuestStatus { NOT_STARTED, STARTED, COMPLETED } var quest_status = QuestStatus.NOT_STARTED var dialog_state = 0 var quest_complete = false

We also need to define some variables that will reference our DialogPopup node in our Player scene, as well as our Player itself — because our player is the one that will initiate the interaction with our NPC. We also need to redefine our Pickups enum, because after a quest has reached its COMPLETED state, we want our player to be awarded with some pickups!

 ### NPC
 #older code
 #reference nodes
 var dialog_popup var player enum Pickups { AMMO, STAMINA, HEALTH }

I also want us to type in the NPC’s name in the Inspector panel, instead of giving them a constant value like “Joe”. To do this, we can export our variable.

 ### NPC
 #older code
 #npc name
 @export var npc_name = ""

In our *rea*dy() function we need to initialize the references to our player and dialog_popup variables. We also need to set the default animation of our NPC to be “idle_down”.

 ### NPC
 #older code
 #initialize variables
 func _ready(): dialog_popup = get_tree().root.get_node("Main/Player/UI/DialogPopup") player = get_tree().root.get_node("Main/Player") $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down")

Now we can create our dialog() function. This dialog tree is quite long, so I recommend you go ahead and just copy and paste it from below. A dialog tree, or conversation tree, is a gameplay mechanic that runs when a player character interacts with an NPC. In this tree, the player is given a choice of what to say and makes subsequent choices until the conversation ends.

In our dialog tree, our NPC runs our Player through a quest to go and find a recipe book. We have not yet created this recipe book, or quest item, which will call our NPC to notify them that the quest has been completed. If we haven’t gotten this quest item yet, the NPC will remind us to get it. If we’ve gotten this quest item, the NPC will thank us and reward us, and complete the quest.

 ### NPC
 #older code
 #dialog tree  func dialog(response = ""): # Set our NPC's animation to "talk"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("talk_down") # Set dialog_popup npc to be referencing this npc
 dialog_popup.npc = self dialog_popup.npc_name = str(npc_name) # dialog tree
 match quest_status: QuestStatus.NOT_STARTED: match dialog_state: 0: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 1 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "Howdy Partner. I haven't seen anybody round these parts in quite a while. That reminds me, I recently lost my momma's secret recipe book, can you help me find it?" dialog_popup.response = "[A] Yes [B] No" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog
 1: match response: "A": # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 2 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "That's mighty kind of you, thanks." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog
 "B": # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 3 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "Well, I'll be waiting like a tumbleweed 'till you come back." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() #re-open to show next dialog
 2: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 0 quest_status = QuestStatus.STARTED # Close dialog popup
 dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") 3: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup
 dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") QuestStatus.STARTED: match dialog_state: 0: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 1 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "Found that book yet?" if quest_complete: dialog_popup.response = "[A] Yes [B] No" else: dialog_popup.response = "[A] No" dialog_popup.open() 1: if quest_complete and response == "A": # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 2 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "Yeehaw! Now I can make cat-eye soup. Here, take this." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() else: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 3 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "I'm so hungry, please hurry..." dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() 2: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 0 quest_status = QuestStatus.COMPLETED # Close dialog popup
 dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") # Add potion and XP to the player.  player.add_pickup(Pickups.AMMO) player.update_xp(50) 3: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup
 dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down") QuestStatus.COMPLETED: match dialog_state: 0: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 1 # Show dialog popup
 dialog_popup.message = "Nice seeing you again partner!" dialog_popup.response = "[A] Bye" dialog_popup.open() 1: # Update dialog tree state
 dialog_state = 0 # Close dialog popup
 dialog_popup.close() # Set NPC's animation back to "idle"
 $AnimatedSprite2D.play("idle_down")

Figure 1: Visual Representation of our Dialog Tree.

Quest Item Setup

For this quest item, we can duplicate our Pickups scene and rename it to “QuestItem”. Detach the Pickups script and signal from the newly created scene and rename its root to “QuestItem”.

Change its sprite to be anything you want. Since our NPC is looking for a recipe book, I’m going to change my sprite to “book_02d.png”, which can be found under the Assets > Icons directory.

Attach a new script to the root of this scene and save it underneath your Scripts folder. Also, connect its body_entered() signal to this new script.

In this script, we need to get a reference to our NPC scene which we will instance in our Main scene. From this, we’ll need to see if the Player has entered the body of this scene, and if true, we can call our to our NPC to change its quest status to complete — since we’ve found the requirements to complete the quest.

 ### QuestItem.gd
 extends Area2D #npc node reference
 var npc func _ready(): npc = get_tree().root.get_node("Main/NPC") #if the player enters the collision body, destroy item and update quest
 func _on_body_entered(body): if body.name == "Player": print("Quest item obtained!") get_tree().queue_delete(self) npc.quest_complete = true

Let’s instance our NPC and our Quest Item in our Main scene.

Here is an example of my map’s layout for my quest:

Now we need to go back to our Player scene so that we can update our interact input to call the dialog function in our NPC script.

 ### Player.gd
 #older code
 #plays attacking animation
 func _input(event): #input event for our attacking, i.e. our shooting
 if event.is_action_pressed("ui_attack"): #checks the current time as the amount of time passed in milliseconds since the engine started
 var now = Time.get_ticks_msec() #check if player can shoot if the reload time has passed and we have ammo
 if now >= bullet_fired_time and ammo_pickup > 0: #shooting anim
 is_attacking = true var animation = "attack_" + returned_direction(new_direction) $AnimatedSprite2D.play(animation) #bullet fired time time to current time
 bullet_fired_time = now + bullet_reload_time #reduce and signal ammo change
 ammo_pickup = ammo_pickup - 1 ammo_pickups_updated.emit(ammo_pickup) #using health consumables
 elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_health"): if health > 0 && health_pickup > 0: health_pickup = health_pickup - 1 health = min(health + 50, max_health) health_updated.emit(health, max_health) health_pickups_updated.emit(health_pickup) #using stamina consumables  elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_consume_stamina"): if stamina > 0 && stamina_pickup > 0: stamina_pickup = stamina_pickup - 1 stamina = min(stamina + 50, max_stamina) stamina_updated.emit(stamina, max_stamina) stamina_pickups_updated.emit(stamina_pickup) #interact with world  elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_interact"): var target = $RayCast2D.get_collider() if target != null: if target.is_in_group("NPC"): # Talk to NPC
 target.dialog() return

We also need to add our dialog functions to our DialogPopup’s input code to capture our A and B responses.

 ### DialogPopup.gd
 #older code
 func _input(event): if event is InputEventKey: if event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_A: npc.dialog("A") elif event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_B: npc.dialog("B")

So now if our player were to run into our NPC and press TAB, the dialog popup should be made visible. We can then navigate through the conversation with our NPC by responding with our “A” for yes and “B” for no keys. If we get the quest item and we go back to the NPC, the dialog options should update, and we should receive our Pickups as a reward. We’ve then reached the end of our dialog tree, so if we return to our NPC, the last line will play.

Before we can test this out, we need to change our node’s process mode, because the DialogPopup will pause the game. Change the NPC’s process mode to “Always” because we need their animations to play even if the game is paused — which is when the dialog is playing.

Change the DialogPopup’s process mode to “When Paused”, because we need to be able to run our input when the game is paused.

Your code should now look like this.

If you run your game, and you run over to your NPC and press “TAB”, your NPC dialog tree should run, and you should be able to accept the quest. If you then run over the quest item and return to your NPC, your NPC will notice that you’ve gotten your quest item and completed the quest. Congratulations, you now have an NPC with a simple quest!

Unfortunately, our quest and dialog are directly tied to our NPC — and in a real game, you would have the NPC, Quest, and Dialog system in their scripts. I’m going to make a separate tutorial series on this, but for now, I just wanted to show you the basics of dialog trees and states.

If you want multiple NPCs, you’ll have to duplicate the NPC scene and script, as well as the Quest Item scene and script — and then just update the values to have unique dialogs and references to your second NPC. I’ll include an example of another NPC in the code reference above.

Troubleshooting: My Dialog Popup is Freezing My Game!

If you’re seeing this issue, it’s because our open() function is pausing our game, and it is preventing our animation from finishing and our player from putting in any input. Also, it’s not because our paused state is assigned first in our open() function — I’ve already tested it, and it doesn’t matter if you pause the game before or after your animation and popup opens. This code works in Godot 4.02 and below, but not in Godot 4.03. To fix this, we have to make some changes to our DialogPopup code.

First, we’ll need to change our Player’s process mode to “Always”. This means our player will be able to add input to the game even when the game is paused.

A problem will arise if we now play our game because our player will be able to walk away from the NPC when the dialog runs, so to fix this, we need to disable our player’s movement that is executed in their physics_process() function. To do this, we can simply call it at the end of our open() function and set its processing to false! Then in our close function, we just need to set it back to true because if the dialog popup is hidden, we want our player to move again.

####DialogPopup.gd

extends Popup

#gets the values of our npc from our NPC scene and sets it in the label values
var npc_name : set = npc_name_set
var message: set = message_set
var response: set = response_set

#reference to NPC
var npc

#sets the npc name with the value received from NPC
func npc_name_set(new_value):
    npc_name = new_value
    $Dialog/NPC.text = new_value

#sets the message with the value received from NPC
func message_set(new_value):
    message = new_value
    $Dialog/Message.text = new_value

#sets the response with the value received from NPC
func response_set(new_value):
    response = new_value
    $Dialog/Response.text = new_value

#no input on hidden
func _ready():
    set_process_input(false)

#opens the dialog
func open():
    get_tree().paused = true
    popup()
    $"../../AnimationPlayer".play("typewriter")
    $"../..".set_physics_process(false)

func _input(event):
    if event is InputEventKey:
        if event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_A:    
            npc.dialog("A")
        elif event.pressed and event.keycode == KEY_B:
            npc.dialog("B")

#closes the dialog  
func close():
    set_process_input(false)
    get_tree().paused = false
    hide()
    $"../..".set_physics_process(true)

#receive input after animation plays    
func _on_animation_player_animation_finished(anim_name):
    set_process_input(true)

And there you have it! Now your game has an NPC with a Quest. The rest of the features that we’ll add from here on will be quick to implement. Most of the hard work is done, so now we just need to add a scene-transition ability to transport our player between worlds, and we’ll also give our player the ability to sleep to restore their health and stamina values in the next part! Remember to save your project, and I’ll see you in the next part.

TUTORIAL RELEASE

LINKS TO OTHER PARTS

The tutorial series has 23 chapters. I’ll be releasing all of the chapters in sectional daily parts over the next couple of weeks. You can find the updated list of the tutorial links for all 23 parts in this series here.

FULL TUTORIAL

If you like this series or want to skip the wait and access the offline, full version of the tutorial series, you can support me by buying the offline booklet for just $3 on Ko-fi!😊

Items.Title: 为什么云技能需求量很大 | Why Cloud Skills are in High Demand
Items.Description:
介绍

云计算彻底改变了数字时代企业的运营方式。 随着组织意识到这项技术的巨大潜力，对具有云技能的专业人员的需求猛增。 在本文中，我们将深入探讨云技能高需求背后的原因，探讨其对就业市场的影响，并阐明获得这些技能的好处。

云计算的兴起

在过去的十年中，云计算已经成为 IT 行业的游戏规则改变者。 云提供了灵活且可扩展的基础架构，使企业能够远程存储、管理和访问数据和应用程序。 该技术消除了对昂贵的本地基础设施的需求，并为创新和增长提供了安全高效的平台。

云技能：职业成功的关键

随着企业越来越多地采用云技术，对云计算专业人员的需求激增。 公司正在寻找能够设计、部署和维护基于云的解决方案以优化其运营的人才。 拥有云技能不仅可以打开一个就业机会的世界，还可以提高您在竞争激烈的就业市场中作为专业人士的价值。

获得云技能的好处

丰厚的工作前景：随着云计算的指数级增长，对具有云技能的专业人员的需求量很大。 就业市场提供了大量高薪职位，包括云架构师、云工程师和云安全专家。

职业发展：获得云技能可以显着促进您的职业发展。 当组织将其运营迁移到云时，他们需要能够驾驭复杂的云环境并确保无缝集成的专家。 拥有云技能可以让您具备承担具有挑战性的角色并在职业生涯中取得进步的知识和专业知识。

多功能性和灵活性：云技能具有高度通用性，适用于各个行业。 无论您从事金融、医疗保健还是电子商务行业，云计算都是优化流程和推动创新不可或缺的一部分。 通过获取云技能，您可以灵活地探索多样化的工作机会并适应不断变化的行业趋势。

创新和效率：云计算使企业能够快速创新并更有效地提供服务。 凭借云技能，您可以利用人工智能和机器学习等尖端技术来开发创新解决方案并简化业务运营。

对就业市场的影响

对云技能的需求不断增长改变了就业市场，造成了需要填补的人才缺口。 随着越来越多的组织迁移到云，缺乏合格的专业人员来满足这一需求。 这种人才缺口为个人提供了一个重要的机会，可以提高技能并在云计算领域从事有价值的职业。

为什么云技能需求量很大

数字化转型：组织的数字化转型之旅在很大程度上依赖于云技术。 对于希望实现基础设施现代化、提高敏捷性并在数字环境中保持竞争力的组织来说，云技能至关重要。

成本优化：云计算为企业提供经济高效的解决方案，无需在硬件和软件方面进行大量前期投资。 云技能使组织能够通过有效管理云资源和实施成本节约策略来优化成本。

可扩展性和灵活性：云技术提供可扩展性和灵活性，允许企业根据需求扩大或缩小其运营规模。 云技能使专业人员能够设计和实施可适应不断变化的业务需求的可扩展解决方案。

数据安全与合规性：随着数据成为企业的命脉，确保其安全性与合规性至关重要。 云技能包括安全最佳实践和合规性法规的知识，使专业人员能够保护数据并降低风险。

协作和远程工作：云计算支持无缝协作和远程工作，这已成为当今数字工作场所不可或缺的一部分。 拥有云技能的专业人员可以促进团队成员之间的协作，无论地理位置如何，并确保远程工作操作顺利进行。

持续创新：云技术不断发展，推出推动创新的新工具和服务。 组织需要具备云技能的专业人员来利用这些进步并保持在技术的前沿。

经常问的问题

问：云技能仅与 IT 专业人员相关吗？

答：不，云技能不仅限于 IT 专业人员。 云计算的应用遍及各个行业，包括金融、医疗保健、零售等。 来自不同背景的专业人士可以从获取云技能中受益。

问：如何获得云技能？

答：获取云技能的方法有多种。 您可以学习在线课程、认证，或参加由信誉良好的组织提供的研讨会和培训计划。 通过项目和实习获得的实践经验对于获得实用的云技能也很有价值。

答：最流行的云平台是 Amazon Web Services (AWS)、Microsoft Azure 和 Google Cloud Platform (GCP)。 建议从其中一个平台开始并熟练掌握，然后再探索其他平台。

问：云计算职业的基本技能是什么？

答：云计算职业的基本技能包括云架构、基础设施管理、安全性、自动化以及云服务模型的知识，例如基础设施即服务 (IaaS)、平台即服务 (PaaS) 和软件即服务 (软件即服务）。

问：云技能如何提高我的就业能力？

答：由于云技术的采用越来越多，云技能受到雇主的高度追捧。 获得云技能可以展示您利用先进技术并为组织的数字化转型做出贡献的能力，从而增强您的就业能力。

问：云技能的未来前景如何？

答：云技能的未来前景是光明的。 随着越来越多的组织采用云技术，对具有云技能的专业人员的需求将持续增长。 云计算处于技术创新的前沿，为具有适当技能的个人提供丰富的职业机会。

结论

总之，对云技能的需求飙升证明了云计算的变革力量。 获得云技能不仅可以打开一个充满就业机会的世界，还可以使您具备推动创新和适应不断发展的数字环境的能力。 拥抱云计算的潜力，踏上提升您职业前景的旅程。 立即培养您的云技能，走在数字革命的最前沿。 加入我们：钦奈的云计算培训

摘要：由于各行业企业广泛采用云计算，因此对云技能的需求量很大。 获得云技能可以提供丰厚的工作前景、职业发展机会以及创新和提高效率的能力。 云技能对就业市场的影响是巨大的，造成了需要填补的人才缺口。 组织寻求具有云技能的专业人员来领导他们的数字化转型计划并优化他们的运营。 立即开始您的云之旅，开启充满无限可能的世界。



原文:

Introduction

Cloud computing has revolutionized the way businesses operate in the digital age. The demand for professionals with cloud skills is skyrocketing as organizations realize the immense potential of this technology. In this article, we will delve into the reasons behind the high demand for cloud skills, exploring its impact on the job market and shedding light on the benefits of acquiring these skills.

The Rise of Cloud Computing

Over the past decade, cloud computing has emerged as a game-changer in the IT industry. The cloud offers a flexible and scalable infrastructure that enables businesses to store, manage, and access data and applications remotely. This technology eliminates the need for costly on-premises infrastructure and provides a secure and efficient platform for innovation and growth.

Cloud Skills: A Key to Career Success

As businesses increasingly adopt cloud technologies, the demand for professionals skilled in cloud computing has surged. Companies are seeking individuals who can design, deploy, and maintain cloud-based solutions to optimize their operations. Having cloud skills not only opens up a world of job opportunities but also enhances your value as a professional in the competitive job market.

Benefits of Acquiring Cloud Skills

Lucrative Job Prospects: With the exponential growth of cloud computing, professionals with cloud skills are in high demand. The job market offers a plethora of well-paying positions, including cloud architects, cloud engineers, and cloud security specialists.

Career Growth: Acquiring cloud skills can significantly boost your career growth. As organizations migrate their operations to the cloud, they require experts who can navigate complex cloud environments and ensure seamless integration. Possessing cloud skills equips you with the knowledge and expertise to take on challenging roles and advance in your career.

Versatility and Flexibility: Cloud skills are highly versatile and applicable across various industries. Whether you're in finance, healthcare, or e-commerce, cloud computing is integral to optimizing processes and driving innovation. By acquiring cloud skills, you gain the flexibility to explore diverse job opportunities and adapt to evolving industry trends.

Innovation and Efficiency: Cloud computing enables businesses to innovate rapidly and deliver services more efficiently. With cloud skills, you can leverage cutting-edge technologies, such as artificial intelligence and machine learning, to develop innovative solutions and streamline business operations.

The Impact on the Job Market

The rising demand for cloud skills has transformed the job market, creating a talent gap that needs to be filled. As more organizations migrate to the cloud, there is a shortage of qualified professionals to meet this demand. This talent gap presents a significant opportunity for individuals to upskill and embark on a rewarding career in cloud computing.

Why Cloud Skills are in High Demand

Digital Transformation: The digital transformation journey of organizations relies heavily on cloud technologies. Cloud skills are essential for organizations looking to modernize their infrastructure, improve agility, and stay competitive in the digital landscape.

Cost Optimization: Cloud computing offers cost-effective solutions for businesses, eliminating the need for large upfront investments in hardware and software. Cloud skills enable organizations to optimize costs by efficiently managing cloud resources and implementing cost-saving strategies.

Scalability and Flexibility: Cloud technologies provide scalability and flexibility, allowing businesses to scale their operations up or down based on demand. Cloud skills empower professionals to design and implement scalable solutions that can adapt to changing business needs.

Data Security and Compliance: As data becomes the lifeblood of businesses, ensuring its security and compliance is of utmost importance. Cloud skills encompass knowledge of security best practices and compliance regulations, enabling professionals to protect data and mitigate risks.

Collaboration and Remote Work: Cloud computing enables seamless collaboration and remote work, which have become integral in today's digital workplace. Professionals with cloud skills can facilitate collaboration among team members, regardless of geographical locations, and ensure smooth remote work operations.

Continuous Innovation: Cloud technologies constantly evolve, introducing new tools and services that drive innovation. Organizations require professionals with cloud skills to leverage these advancements and stay at the forefront of technology.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Are cloud skills only relevant to IT professionals?

A: No, cloud skills are not limited to IT professionals. Cloud computing has applications across various industries, including finance, healthcare, retail, and more. Professionals from diverse backgrounds can benefit from acquiring cloud skills.

Q: How can I acquire cloud skills?

A: There are several ways to acquire cloud skills. You can pursue online courses, certifications, or attend workshops and training programs offered by reputable organizations. Hands-on experience through projects and internships is also valuable for gaining practical cloud skills.

Q: Which cloud platforms should I focus on learning?

A: The most popular cloud platforms are Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). It is advisable to start with one of these platforms and gain proficiency before exploring others.

Q: What are the essential skills for a career in cloud computing?

A: Essential skills for a career in cloud computing include cloud architecture, infrastructure management, security, automation, and knowledge of cloud service models like Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), and Software as a Service (SaaS).

Q: How can cloud skills enhance my employability?

A: Cloud skills are highly sought after by employers due to the increasing adoption of cloud technologies. Acquiring cloud skills enhances your employability by demonstrating your ability to leverage advanced technologies and contribute to an organization's digital transformation.

Q: What is the future outlook for cloud skills?

A: The future outlook for cloud skills is promising. As more organizations embrace cloud technologies, the demand for professionals with cloud skills will continue to grow. Cloud computing is at the forefront of technological innovation, offering abundant career opportunities for individuals with the right skills.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the soaring demand for cloud skills is a testament to the transformative power of cloud computing. Acquiring cloud skills not only opens up a world of job opportunities but also equips you with the ability to drive innovation and adapt to the evolving digital landscape. Embrace the potential of cloud computing and embark on a journey to enhance your career prospects. Develop your cloud skills today and be at the forefront of the digital revolution. Join Us: Cloud Computing Training in Chennai

Summary: Cloud skills are in high demand due to the widespread adoption of cloud computing by businesses across industries. Acquiring cloud skills offers lucrative job prospects, career growth opportunities, and the ability to innovate and drive efficiency. The impact of cloud skills on the job market is significant, creating a talent gap that needs to be filled. Organizations seek professionals with cloud skills to lead their digital transformation initiatives and optimize their operations. Start your cloud journey today and unlock a world of possibilities.

Items.Title: 欢迎来到应用程序开发的新时代：Avalonia v11 简介 | Welcome to the New Era of App Development: Introducing Avalonia v11
Items.Description:
今天，我们很高兴地宣布 Avalonia 11.0 发布！

经过近一年的预览和无数个小时的奉献，是时候释放跨平台应用程序开发的游戏规则改变者了。 是什么让 Avalonia v11 成为我们旅程中的里程碑？ 以下是我们在这个革命性版本中包含的丰富功能：

新平台拓宽视野

扩大的平台支持是 v11 中最重要的更新之一。 在此版本中，我们添加了 iOS、Android 和 WebAssembly 支持，将我们的跨平台功能提升到了新的水平。

展望未来，我们还预览了对Apple VisionOS 的支持，并看到我们令人难以置信的社区工作，以在三星 TizenOS上启用 Avalonia UI！ 我们独特的架构使我们能够轻松添加新平台，这是我们去年写的。

新的合成渲染器

我们的新合成渲染器在视觉功能和性能方面取得了重大进步。 从本质上讲，合成渲染器带来了新的动画可能性。 通过对隐式动画、连接动画和渲染线程动画的支持，您的应用程序的视觉活力可以达到新的高度。 这些功能可实现流畅、高性能的动画，从而丰富您的用户界面并提供引人入胜的用户体验。

除了动画增强之外，合成渲染器还提供了显着的性能改进。 它的设计目标是高效，减少内存压力，即使在低功耗嵌入式设备上也能实现高帧速率。 即使在已有 20 年历史的嵌入式 CPU 上运行，我们的架构也能提供无缝的用户体验。 这种级别的性能优化非常罕见，这证明了 Avalonia 能够适应不同的硬件配置，同时保持应用程序的流畅、响应灵敏。

高级文本渲染和布局

我们引入了支持内联元素的富文本渲染。 此功能允许在单个 TextBlock 中进行巨大的变化，从更改字体样式到添加超链接，甚至嵌入控件 - 这是以前无法实现的。

这些改进和功能为完整的富文本编辑奠定了基础，我们很高兴在即将推出的 Avalonia 版本中探索这一功能。

输入法支持

我们对全球影响力和包容性的承诺导致在 v11 中引入了输入法编辑器(IME) 支持。 这一重要功能可帮助用户输入其输入设备上找不到的字符和符号。 这意味着 Avalonia 应用程序现在可以很好地配合自动完成和其他操作系统提供的文本功能等功能。 因此，Avalonia 应用程序变得更加用户友好，并且能够适应各种输入法。

辅助功能支持

我们相信技术应该具有包容性并可供所有人使用。 因此，我们很高兴推出新的辅助功能支持，这确保每个人（包括残疾人）都可以使用和享受 Avalonia 应用程序。

我们为全面的辅助功能支持奠定了基础，实施了一些关键功能，使有视觉、运动或认知障碍的用户更容易使用应用程序。 这些增强功能符合行业标准和准则，使 Avalonia 成为关注构建可访问软件的开发人员更具吸引力的选择。

我们在键盘导航、屏幕阅读器支持和高对比度主题方面取得了长足进步。 虽然我们为自己的进步感到自豪，但我们认为这仅仅是一个开始。 我们致力于不断提高 Avalonia 的可访问性，让每个人都能更轻松地使用使用我们框架构建的应用程序。

自动化测试

我们彻底改进了自动化测试功能，以确保您可以轻松开发强大且可靠的应用程序。 此版本引入了对自动化测试的支持，该测试扩展到 Avalonia 测试环境之外，还包括Appium等传统测试框架。 借助 v11，您可以利用您已经熟悉的测试工具来确保您的应用程序跨平台完美运行。

但我们的测试能力的增强甚至更进一步。 通过引入“无头”模式，您可以运行整个应用程序并以令人难以置信的高速对其执行测试。 这种无头模式简化了测试过程，使您能够比以往更有效地验证功能并检测错误。 这是我们测试能力的巨大飞跃，我们相信它将极大地改善您的开发工作流程。

控制主题

虽然我们一直对 Avalonia 提供的可定制性感到自豪，但在这个版本中，我们更进一步为开发人员提供了对其应用程序外观和感觉的更大灵活性和控制力。

这种轻松的定制还提高了效率，使您能够以更少的时间和精力实现所需的美感。

此外，我们还改进了对主题切换的支持，允许您的用户在浅色和深色模式或您提供的其他自定义主题之间无缝、动态地切换。

我们很高兴地宣布 v11 完全支持提前 (AOT) 编译和修剪，解决了以前版本中的问题。 更新的修剪功能消除了发布过程中未使用的代码，从而大大减小了应用程序的大小。 借助 v11，开发人员现在可以体验到应用程序大小缩小约 60%，从而提供更快的下载速度、更快的启动时间以及更少的最终用户存储使用量。

修改后的开发者文档

您的反馈非常宝贵，我们已经清楚地听到您关于改进我们的文档的声音！ 我们很高兴地宣布，我们已经将文档从 GitBook 转移到了新的自定义平台，该平台可以更准确地满足我们不断变化的需求。

这一重大进步为我们未来的增强提供了巨大的灵活性。 我们的目标是不断采用和完善文档，以更好地服务于我们不断扩大的社区。

我们还致力于创建快速指南，这些指南是演示特定功能的重点示例应用程序。

我们希望看到社区更多的参与来帮助我们改进文档。 这并不一定意味着您自己编写文档 - 尽管这会受到欢迎。 同样重要的是分享您的经验。 告诉我们您遇到障碍的领域，或者我们当前的文档在支持您的旅程方面存在不足的地方。 这种反馈非常有价值，可以帮助我们了解改进工作的重点。 每一项贡献，无论大小，都有助于将 Avalonia 完善为对开发人员更加友好的框架。

模板工作室

我们发布了Template Studio for Avalonia的第一个版本 - 一个简化的解决方案，旨在使用直观的、基于向导的 UI 加速新 Avalonia 应用程序的创建。

开始使用 Template Studio for Avalonia 非常简单。 只需安装扩展并在 Visual Studio 中创建新项目时选择 Template Studio 项目模板即可。

生态系统的增长

我们有幸与一系列专门为 Avalonia 开发的专业控制供应商密切合作。

这标志着 Avalonia 发展历程中的一个重要里程碑，因为它标志着更广泛的生态系统的转变 - 吸引了之前致力于支持 WPF、Xamarin 和其他 Microsoft UI 工具包的供应商的商业兴趣。

我们已经为我们的下一组 UI 控件检查了各种基于 .NET 的跨平台 UI 框架，但没有一个框架能够像 Avalonia 那样捕捉 WPF 的魔力。 观察这种增长并成为推动这种增长的充满活力的社区的一部分是令人兴奋和谦卑的。 我们非常感谢这些供应商的合作以及他们对 Avalonia 潜力的信任。 随着我们的前进，我们对未来的合作和 Avalonia 生态系统的持续发展充满热情。

谢谢你！

在过去的十年里，全球300 多名贡献者将 Avalonia 塑造成了今天的样子。 它体现了我们充满活力的社区的集体辛勤工作和创建最佳跨平台 UI 框架的雄心。

我们邀请您加入我们这个激动人心的旅程，帮助塑造 Avalonia 的下一个十年和跨平台 .NET 应用程序开发的未来。

入门

准备好深入了解 Avalonia v11 并探索所有令人惊叹的新功能了吗？ 请参阅我们的入门指南，开始使用 Avalonia 进行构建。

如果您已经在使用 Avalonia 进行开发并想要升级到 v11，请查看我们的升级指南。



原文:

Today, we are thrilled to announce the release of Avalonia 11.0!

After nearly a year in preview and countless thousands of hours of dedication, it is time to unleash a game-changer in cross-platform application development. Avalonia v11 embodies a huge leap forward in our goal to provide developers with an efficient, feature-rich, and seamless experience to create exceptional UIs across all major platforms.

What makes Avalonia v11 a milestone in our journey? Here's a peek into the wealth of features we have packed into this revolutionary version:

Broadened Horizons with New Platforms

The broadened platform support is one of the most significant updates in v11. With this release, we've included iOS, Android, and WebAssembly support, taking our cross-platform capabilities to new horizons.

Looking to the future, we’ve also previewed support for Apple's visionOS and seen our incredible community work to enable Avalonia UI on Samsung's TizenOS! Our unique architecture enables us to add new platforms with ease, which we wrote about last year.

New Composition Renderer

Our new composition renderer is a significant advancement in visual capability and performance. At its core, the composition renderer brings new animation possibilities to the table. With support for implicit animations, connected animations and render thread animations, the visual dynamism of your application can reach new heights. These features allow for smooth, high-performance animations that can enrich your user interfaces and provide engaging user experiences.

Beyond the animation enhancements, the composition renderer provides substantial performance improvements. It’s designed to be highly efficient, reducing memory pressure and enabling high frame rates even on low-powered embedded devices. A testament to Avalonia's efficiency is its widespread use in embedded systems. Our architecture enables delivering a seamless user experience, even when operating on 20-year-old embedded CPUs. This level of performance optimization is rare, demonstrating Avalonia's ability to adapt to diverse hardware configurations while maintaining smooth, responsive applications.

Advanced Text Rendering and Layout

Avalonia v11 ushers in a new era of text rendering capabilities, significantly enhancing control and flexibility over the visual elements in your UI. We’ve introduced rich text rendering that supports inline elements. This feature allows for dramatic variations in a single TextBlock, from changing font styles to adding hyperlinks, or even embedding controls - something that wasn’t previously achievable.

These improvements and capabilities set the foundation for full rich text editing, a feature we’re excited to explore in the upcoming versions of Avalonia.

IME Support

Our commitment to global reach and inclusivity has led to the introduction of Input Method Editor (IME) support in v11. This vital feature assists users in entering characters and symbols not found on their input devices. IME support also enables seamless integration with on-screen keyboards on mobile and web platforms. This means that Avalonia apps now play nicely with features such as auto-completions and other OS provided text features. As a result, Avalonia apps are become even more user-friendly and adaptable to a range of input methods.

Accessibility Support

We believe that technology should be inclusive and accessible to all. Therefore, we're excited to introduce our new accessibility support, which ensures that Avalonia applications can be used and enjoyed by everyone, including those with disabilities.

We've laid the groundwork for full accessibility support, implementing critical features that make apps more accessible to users with visual, motor, or cognitive disabilities. These enhancements comply with industry standards and guidelines, making Avalonia a more attractive choice for developers concerned with building accessible software.

We've made strides in keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and high-contrast themes. And while we're proud of our progress, we consider this to be just the beginning. We're committed to continuously improving the accessibility of Avalonia, making it easier for everyone to use the applications built with our framework.

Automated Testing

We’ve overhauled our automated testing capabilities to ensure you can develop robust and reliable applications with ease. This release introduces support for automated testing that extends beyond the Avalonia test environment to include traditional testing frameworks like Appium. With v11, you can take advantage of the testing tools you’re already familiar with to ensure your application runs flawlessly across platforms.

But the enhancements to our testing capabilities go even further. With the introduction of a ‘Headless’ mode, you can run your entire application and execute tests on it at incredibly high speed. This headless mode streamlines the testing process, enabling you to validate functionality and detect bugs more efficiently than ever before. This is a monumental leap forward in our testing capabilities, and we believe it will dramatically improve your development workflow.

Control Themes

One of the notable enhancements in v11 is the significant improvements made to our control themes. While we've always prided ourselves on the customizability offered by Avalonia, in this version, we've taken it a step further to provide developers with even more flexibility and control over the look and feel of their applications.

Control themes in v11 have been restructured for easier customization, which is particularly beneficial if you have a precise, consistent visual identity you'd like to maintain across your applications. This ease of customization also increases efficiency, enabling you to achieve the desired aesthetic with less time and effort.

Additionally, we've improved our support for theme switching, allowing your users to switch between light and dark modes or other custom themes you provide seamlessly and dynamically.

AOT & Trimming

We're excited to announce that v11 fully supports Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation and trimming, resolving issues from previous versions. The updated trimming functionality eliminates unused code during the publishing process, dramatically reducing application sizes. With v11, developers can now experience ~60% smaller application sizes, providing faster downloads, quicker startup times, and less end-user storage used.

Revamped Developer Documentation

Your feedback has been invaluable, and we’ve heard you loud and clear about improving our documentation! We’re thrilled to reveal that we’ve transitioned our documentation from GitBook to a new custom platform that aligns more precisely with our evolving needs.

This significant step forward offers us enormous flexibility for future enhancements. The goal is to continually adopt and refine the documentation to better serve our expanding community.

We’ve also worked on creating quick-guides, which are focused example applications that demonstrate specific functionalities.

We hope to see more participation from the community to help us improve documentation. This doesn’t necessarily mean writing documentation yourself - although that would be welcome. Equally important is sharing your experiences. Tell us about the areas where you encountered hurdles, or where our current documentation fell short in supporting your journey. This kind of feedback is immensely valuable and helps us understand where to focus our improvement efforts. Every contribution, whether big or small, goes a long way in refining Avalonia into an even more developer-friendly framework.

Template Studio

We’ve released our first version of Template Studio for Avalonia - a streamlined solution designed to accelerate the creation of new Avalonia apps using an intuitive, wizard-based UI.

Getting started with Template Studio for Avalonia is a breeze. Simply install the extension and select the Template Studio project template when creating a new project in Visual Studio.

Ecosystem Growth

We've had the privilege of working closely with a range of pro-control vendors who have been developing specifically for Avalonia.

This marks an important milestone in Avalonia's journey as it signals a shift in the broader ecosystem - attracting commercial interest from vendors who have previously dedicated their efforts to supporting WPF, Xamarin and other Microsoft UI toolkits.

We’ve examined various .NET-based cross-platform UI frameworks for our next set of UI controls, but none of them capture the magic of WPF in the way that Avalonia does. Avalonia has a very strong community, offers pixel-perfect rendering on a multitude of platforms, is easy to customize, and frankly is a joy to work with.
Bill Henning, President, Actipro Software

It's exciting and humbling to observe this growth and be part of the vibrant community that is driving it. We're immensely grateful for the collaboration of these vendors and their belief in Avalonia's potential. As we move forward, we're enthusiastic about future collaborations and the continuing growth of Avalonia's ecosystem.

Thank you!

Avalonia has always been a labour of love, a community-driven project open to all. Over the past decade, more than 300 contributors worldwide have shaped Avalonia into what it is today. Avalonia v11 is not just a new version; it embodies our vibrant community's collective hard work and ambition to create the best cross-platform UI framework.

We invite you to join us on this exciting journey and help shape the next decade of Avalonia and the future of cross-platform .NET app development.

Getting Started

Ready to dive into Avalonia v11 and explore all the amazing new features? Head over to our Get Started guide to begin building with Avalonia.

If you’re already developing with Avalonia and want to upgrade to v11 then check out our upgrade guide.

Items.Title: 移动应用程序维护终极指南：最佳实践和技巧 | The Ultimate Guide to Mobile App Maintenance: Best Practices and Tips
Items.Description:
介绍

在当今快速发展的数字环境中，移动应用程序已成为我们生活中不可或缺的一部分。 它们是企业吸引客户、简化流程和提高生产力的强大工具。 然而，开发成功的移动应用程序只是第一步。 为了确保其使用寿命和性能，正确的维护至关重要。
在这份综合指南中，我们将探讨移动应用程序维护的最佳实践和技巧，使您能够优化应用程序的性能并提供卓越的用户体验。

移动应用程序维护指南

定期更新和错误修复

移动应用程序维护的主要方面之一是定期更新和错误修复。 随着技术的进步和操作系统的发展，让您的应用程序保持最新状态至关重要。
定期更新不仅确保与最新操作系统版本的兼容性，而且还提供引入新功能和增强功能的机会。

错误修复对于维持无缝的用户体验同样重要。 通过严格的测试和用户反馈分析，及时发现并解决任何错误或问题。 根据错误修复对用户体验和业务目标的影响确定其优先级。

性能优化

移动应用程序性能在用户满意度和保留率方面发挥着重要作用。 用户期望应用程序能够快速加载、迅速响应并且运行流畅。 要优化性能，请考虑以下事项：

优化代码：检查并优化应用程序的代码，以消除任何冗余或低效的流程。 最大限度地减少资源使用、减少加载时间并提高整体性能。

图像和文件优化：压缩图像和文件以减小其大小而不影响质量。 这种优化技术显着缩短了加载时间，特别是对于互联网连接速度较慢的用户而言。

缓存机制：实现缓存机制，将经常访问的数据存储在本地。 缓存可以减少服务器请求并增强应用程序性能，即使在离线模式下也是如此。

用户反馈和评论

与用户互动并积极寻求他们的反馈是移动应用程序维护中的宝贵实践。 鼓励用户通过应用内提示或电子邮件活动提供反馈和评论。
分析此反馈以识别重复出现的问题、痛点和功能请求。 优先考虑用户反馈并相应地调整您的维护工作。

安全和数据保护

维持强大的安全措施对于保护用户数据和维持用户的信任至关重要。 定期更新应用程序的安全功能，包括加密协议、安全身份验证机制和数据存储实践。 随时了解新出现的安全威胁，并通过补丁或更新及时解决任何漏洞。

兼容性和平台更新

操作系统更新带来了新功能、增强的安全性和性能改进。 为了确保您的应用程序与最新的操作系统版本保持兼容，请随时了解特定于平台的指南和要求。
定期在不同设备和操作系统版本上测试您的应用程序，以识别兼容性问题。 及时解决这些问题以避免负面的用户体验。

监控和分析

利用应用程序分析和监控工具来深入了解用户行为、应用程序性能和潜在问题。 监控关键指标，例如用户参与度、保留率、崩溃和加载时间。
识别模式，发现潜在瓶颈，并使用数据驱动的见解来做出明智的决策，以优化应用程序的维护策略。

灾难恢复和备份

通过实施强大的灾难恢复和备份计划，为服务器故障或数据泄露等不可预见的事件做好准备。
定期备份应用程序的数据并确保恢复机制到位以快速恢复功能。 定期测试您的灾难恢复计划以确保其有效性。

测试和质量保证

彻底的测试和质量保证是移动应用程序维护的重要方面。 实施全面的测试流程，包括功能、性能、安全和兼容性测试。
自动化测试工具可以帮助简化此过程，确保应用程序在各种设备和场景中按预期执行。

结论

Introduction

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, mobile apps have become an integral part of our lives. They serve as powerful tools for businesses to engage customers, streamline processes, and boost productivity. However, developing a successful mobile app is just the first step. To ensure its longevity and performance, proper maintenance is crucial.
In this comprehensive guide, we will explore the best practices and tips for mobile app maintenance, empowering you to optimize your app's performance and provide an exceptional user experience.

Guide to Mobile App Maintenance

Regular Updates and Bug Fixes

One of the primary aspects of mobile app maintenance is regular updates and bug fixes. As technology advances and operating systems evolve, it's essential to keep your app up to date.
Regular updates not only ensure compatibility with the latest OS versions but also provide an opportunity to introduce new features and enhancements.

Bug fixes are equally vital in maintaining a seamless user experience. Through rigorous testing and user feedback analysis, identify and address any bugs or issues promptly. Prioritize bug fixes based on their impact on user experience and business objectives.

Performance Optimization

Mobile app performance plays a significant role in user satisfaction and retention. Users expect apps to load quickly, respond promptly, and operate smoothly. To optimize performance, consider the following:

a. Optimize Code: Review and optimize your app's code to eliminate any redundant or inefficient processes. Minimize resource usage, reduce load times, and enhance overall performance.

b. Image and File Optimization: Compress images and files to reduce their size without compromising quality. This optimization technique significantly improves loading times, especially for users with slower internet connections.

c. Caching Mechanism: Implement a caching mechanism to store frequently accessed data locally. Caching reduces server requests and enhances app performance, even in offline mode.

User Feedback and Reviews

Engaging with users and actively seeking their feedback is an invaluable practice in mobile app maintenance. Encourage users to provide feedback and reviews through in-app prompts or email campaigns.
Analyze this feedback to identify recurring issues, pain points, and feature requests. Prioritize user feedback and align your maintenance efforts accordingly.

Security and Data Protection

Maintaining robust security measures is crucial for protecting user data and maintaining users' trust. Regularly update your app's security features, including encryption protocols, secure authentication mechanisms, and data storage practices. Stay informed about emerging security threats and promptly address any vulnerabilities through patches or updates.

Compatibility and Platform Updates

Operating system updates bring new features, enhanced security, and performance improvements. To ensure your app remains compatible with the latest OS versions, stay updated with platform-specific guidelines and requirements.
Regularly test your app on different devices and OS versions to identify compatibility issues. Address these issues promptly to avoid negative user experiences.

Monitoring and Analytics

Leverage app analytics and monitoring tools to gain insights into user behavior, app performance, and potential issues. Monitor key metrics such as user engagement, retention rates, crashes, and load times.
Identify patterns, spot potential bottlenecks, and use data-driven insights to make informed decisions for optimizing your app's maintenance strategy.

Disaster Recovery and Backup

Prepare for unforeseen events such as server failures or data breaches by implementing a robust disaster recovery and backup plan.
Regularly back up your app's data and ensure that recovery mechanisms are in place to restore functionality swiftly. Regularly test your disaster recovery plan to ensure its effectiveness.

Testing and Quality Assurance

Thorough testing and quality assurance are vital aspects of mobile app maintenance. Implement comprehensive testing processes, including functional, performance, security, and compatibility testing.
Automated testing tools can help streamline this process, ensuring the app performs as expected across various devices and scenarios.

Conclusion

Mobile app maintenance is an ongoing process that requires dedication, attention to detail, and a commitment to providing an exceptional user experience. By following these best practices and tips, you can ensure the longevity, performance, and security of your mobile app. Regular updates, bug fixes, performance optimization, user feedback analysis, security measures, compatibility testing, and robust disaster recovery plans are all essential components of a successful mobile app maintenance strategy.

For businesses that may lack the expertise or resources to handle app maintenance in-house, seeking professional mobile app maintenance services is a wise decision. Mobile app maintenance service providers specialize in the intricacies of app maintenance and have the necessary tools and knowledge to keep your app running smoothly.

When choosing a mobile app maintenance service, consider their track record, reputation, and expertise in your app's platform. Look for providers that offer comprehensive maintenance packages, including regular updates, bug fixes, performance optimization, security audits, and proactive monitoring.

Outsourcing mobile app maintenance allows your internal team to focus on core business activities while ensuring that your app remains up to date, secure, and efficient. By partnering with a reliable mobile app maintenance service, you can mitigate risks, optimize user experience, and stay ahead of the competition in the ever-evolving mobile app landscape.

Embrace these practices, adapt to evolving user expectations, and consider the benefits of partnering with a trusted mobile app maintenance service. With a solid maintenance strategy in place, your mobile app can thrive, delight users, and contribute to the success of your business.

Items.Title: 克服酒精成瘾：有效治疗的综合指南 | OvercomingOvercoming Alcohol Addiction: A Comprehensive Guide to Effective Treatment
Items.Description:
酒精成瘾是一个无情的敌人，影响着全世界无数人的生命。 但希望在黑暗中闪耀，专门的治疗项目为那些寻求康复的人提供了生命线。 在这篇文章中，我们深入研究酒精成瘾治疗的世界，探索其多方面的组成部分，并帮助个人摆脱成瘾的束缚。

踏上康复之路始于富有同情心的医疗专业人员进行的全面评估。 这一关键步骤可以衡量成瘾的严重程度，识别同时发生的心理健康问题，并制定个性化的治疗计划。 为了确保旅途安全，排毒通常是下一阶段。 在警惕的医疗监督下，身体会被温和地清除酒精，同时控制戒断症状，​​为未来的康复之旅奠定坚实的基础。

康复计划可满足不同的需求，提供住院和门诊选择。 住院治疗为个人提供了结构化和沉浸式的环境，促进了变革。 全天候监督、强化治疗课程和支持性社区使个人能够重写自己的故事。 另外，门诊项目可以灵活地维持日常生活，同时参加预定的治疗课程和支持小组，通过外部支持系统促进康复。

在治疗领域，个体治疗课程是个人成长的强大催化剂。 善解人意的咨询师和心理学家采用循证疗法，例如认知行为疗法（CBT）、动机访谈和辩证行为疗法（DBT）。 这些方法深入研究成瘾的核心原因，解决思维模式和触发因素，并为个人提供宝贵的应对机制。 通过一对一的课程，个人获得了克服渴望、做出更健康的选择并拥抱更光明的未来的工具。

团结才能增强力量，使得团体治疗和支持在康复过程中变得非常宝贵。 小组会议为个人提供了一个与志趣相投的同伴联系的平台，培养归属感和理解感。 在培育团体环境中分享个人故事、胜利和挑战可以提供深远的治疗益处。 戒酒互诫协会 (AA) 等广受认可的计划为持续支持提供了结构化框架，激励个人以新的决心继续前进。

有效的治疗超越了心灵，包括滋养身体、心灵和精神的整体方法。 正念和冥想练习、瑜伽、艺术治疗、锻炼和营养指导创建了一个强大的支持系统。 这些补充干预措施可以促进自我保健、减轻压力和整体健康，从而加速康复过程。 通过采用整体模式，个人可以增强韧性，找到安慰，并点燃内心治愈的火花。

康复是一项终生的承诺，善后护理是长期清醒的重要支柱。 治疗计划优先考虑复发预防策略、出院计划和获得支持网络。 积极参与支持小组、门诊咨询以及定期与医疗保健专业人员进行检查可确保持续的指导并增强复原力。 通过利用善后护理资源，个人可以坚定自己的决心，在与旧病复发的斗争中保持领先一步。

结论

在酒精成瘾的阴影中，有一盏希望的灯塔。 通过包括个体化治疗、团体支持、整体方法和善后护理在内的专门治疗计划，个人可以踏上通往持久康复的变革之旅。 这条道路可能充满挑战，但凭借专业人士的帮助和坚定不移的决心，更光明、无酒精的未来是指日可待的。



原文:

## Introduction
Alcohol addiction is a relentless foe that affects countless lives worldwide. But hope shines through the darkness, as specialized treatment programs offer a lifeline for those seeking recovery. In this article, we delve into the world of alcohol addiction treatment, exploring its multifaceted components and empowering individuals to break free from the chains of addiction.

1. Initial Assessment and Detoxification

Embarking on the path to recovery starts with a thorough assessment conducted by compassionate medical professionals. This crucial step gauges the severity of addiction, identifies co-occurring mental health concerns, and tailors a personalized treatment plan. To ensure a safe journey, detoxification is often the next phase. Under vigilant medical supervision, the body is gently purged of alcohol while managing withdrawal symptoms, laying a solid foundation for the recovery journey ahead.

2. Inpatient or Outpatient Rehabilitation

Rehab programs cater to diverse needs, offering both inpatient and outpatient options. Inpatient treatment provides individuals with a structured and immersive environment, fostering transformative change. Around-the-clock supervision, intensive therapy sessions, and a supportive community empower individuals to rewrite their stories. Alternatively, outpatient programs grant flexibility to maintain daily routines while attending scheduled therapy sessions and support groups, bolstering recovery with external support systems.

3. Individualized Therapy

Within the treatment landscape, individual therapy sessions serve as powerful catalysts for personal growth. Empathetic counselors and psychologists employ evidence-based modalities, such as cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), motivational interviewing, and dialectical behavior therapy (DBT). These approaches delve into the core causes of addiction, addressing thought patterns, and triggers, and equipping individuals with invaluable coping mechanisms. Through one-on-one sessions, individuals gain the tools to conquer cravings, make healthier choices, and embrace a brighter future.

4. Group Support and Peer Interaction

Strength thrives in unity, making group therapy and support invaluable in the recovery process. Group sessions provide individuals with a platform to connect with like-minded peers, fostering a sense of belonging and understanding. Sharing personal stories, triumphs, and challenges within a nurturing group environment offers profound therapeutic benefits. Widely recognized programs like Alcoholics Anonymous (AA) provide a structured framework for continued support, inspiring individuals to forge ahead with newfound determination.

5. Holistic Approaches

Effective treatment goes beyond the mind, encompassing holistic approaches to nurture the body, mind, and spirit. Mindfulness and meditation practices, yoga, art therapy, exercise, and nutritional guidance create a robust support system. These complementary interventions foster self-care, stress reduction, and overall well-being, amplifying the recovery process. By embracing holistic modalities, individuals build resilience, find solace, and ignite a spark of inner healing.

6. Aftercare and Relapse Prevention

Recovery is a lifelong commitment, and aftercare forms an essential pillar of long-term sobriety. Treatment programs prioritize relapse prevention strategies, discharge planning, and access to support networks. Active participation in support groups, outpatient counseling, and regular check-ins with healthcare professionals ensure ongoing guidance and reinforce resilience. By embracing aftercare resources, individuals fortify their resolve, staying one step ahead in the battle against relapse.

Conclusion

Amidst the shadows of alcohol addiction, there is a beacon of hope. With specialized treatment programs encompassing individualized therapy, group support, holistic approaches, and aftercare, individuals can embark on a transformative journey toward lasting recovery. The path may be challenging, but with professional help and unwavering determination, a brighter, alcohol-free future is within reach.

Items.Title: 将 AI 融入您的 React 应用程序 | Incorporating AI Into Your React App
Items.Description:
如果你是一名程序员，你肯定知道 React。 您很可能知道如何使用 React 创建 Web 应用程序。 你可以用 React 创建无穷无尽的东西。 随着这个新时代的到来，我们有了一个新朋友，称为 AI（人工智能）。 现在，如果我们创建一个 React 应用程序，然后将 AI 融入其中会怎样？ 今天这个博客将介绍如何做到这一点。

首先，它并不像人们想象的那么困难。 今天我们将使用 Alan AI 来帮助我们创建一个带有 AI 的 React 应用程序。 这将是一个简单的支持语音的 React 应用程序。 用户将能够单击语音助手按钮并发出命令，作为回报，艾伦人工智能将回复他们。

步骤1.进入Alan AI Studio ( alan.app/ )。 然后注册并在 Alan AI 中创建一个项目。

步骤 2. 现在是时候创建一个简单的 React 应用程序了。

  1. 转到您要在其中创建应用程序的文件夹。
    npx create-react-app my-app

  2. 切换到该文件夹
    cd my-app

步骤 3. 安装 Alan AI Web 组件。 幸运的是，在当今时代，我们不必从头开始创建人工智能。
npm i @alan-ai/alan-sdk-web

步骤 4. 是时候将 Alan AI 按钮添加到应用程序中了

您需要将 YOUR_KEY_FROM_ALAN_STUDIO_HERE 替换为特殊的 Alan AI SDK 密钥。 在 Alan AI studio 中，代码编辑器顶部单击集成，然后复制 Alan SDK key 字段中提供的值，然后将该值粘贴到代码中的“key”。

步骤 5. 最后是添加语音命令的时候了。

 intent(`What is your name?`, p => { p.play(`It's Alan, and yours?`); }); intent(`How are you doing?`, p => { p.play(`Good, thank you. What about you?`); });

现在，在您的 React 应用程序中，您将可以询问“您叫什么名字？” 和“你好吗？”

因为我们有有用的库和框架，所以将 AI 合并到您的 React 应用程序中并不太复杂。 这只是一个框架。 这里还有一些框架和库，因此您可以自己创建 AI React 应用程序。 在这篇博客中，我们介绍了整合人工智能的一种非常基本的方法。 后端和前端开发的结合会变得更加困难。 这只是一种简单有趣的让你的双脚湿漉漉的方式。

我一直觉得 AI 非常有趣，事实上现在可以创建一个令人惊叹的 AI React 应用程序。 正如我之前提到的，这是整合人工智能的一种非常基本的方法，尽管它非常简洁。 作为一名仍在学校的开发人员，我喜欢遇到新的挑战和障碍。 有了这些新信息，我发现还有什么比博客更好的分享方式呢？ 现在去创建一个 AI React 应用程序吧！

原文:

If you are a programmer you definitely know about React. You most likely know how to use React to create a web application. There are endless things you can create with React. Well with this new day and age we have a new friend called AI (Artificial Intelligence). Now what if we created a React application and then incorporated AI into it. Today this blog will be covering how to do so.

First things first it is not as difficult as one would expect. Today we will be using Alan AI to help us create a React app with AI. This will be a simple voice enabled React app. Users will be able to click the voice assistant button and give commands and in return Alan AI will reply to them.

Step 1. Go to Alan AI Studio (alan.app/). Then sign up and in Alan AI create a project.

Step 2. Now it is time to create a simple React app.

  1. Go to the folder where you would like to create the app.
    npx create-react-app my-app

  2. Switch to that folder
    cd my-app

3.Now just start it up
npm start

Step 3. Install the Alan AI Web component. Luckily with this day and age we don't have to create an AI from scratch.
npm i @alan-ai/alan-sdk-web

Step 4. Its time to add the Alan AI button to the app

1.Open your App.js file

2.Import the Alan AI Web component
import alanBtn from "@alan-ai/alan-sdk-web";

3.Use the Effect Hook to add the Alan AI button.
import React, { useEffect } from 'react';

4.Now call useEffect

function App() {
    // Adding the Alan AI button
    useEffect(() => {
        alanBtn({
            key: 'YOUR_KEY_FROM_ALAN_STUDIO_HERE',
            onCommand: (commandData) => {
                if (commandData.command === 'go:back') {
                    // Call the client code that will react to the received command
                }
            }
        });
    }, []);
}
  1. You need to replace YOUR_KEY_FROM_ALAN_STUDIO_HERE with the special Alan AI SDK key. In Alan AI studio at the top of the code editor click Integrations, then copy the value provided in the Alan SDK key field then paste the value to "key" in the code.

Step 5. Finally its time to add voice commands.

intent(`What is your name?`, p => {
    p.play(`It's Alan, and yours?`);
});

intent(`How are you doing?`, p => {
    p.play(`Good, thank you. What about you?`);
});

Now in your react app you will be able to ask "What is your name?" and "How are you doing?"

See since we have helpful libraries and frameworks it is not too complicated to incorporate AI into your react app. This is just one framework. Here are a couple more frameworks and libraries so you can go and create an AI react app on your own. TensorFlow.js, Brain.js, OpenCV.js, IBM Watson Developer Cloud, ML5.js, Microsoft Cognitive Services. In this blog we went over a very basic way to incorporate AI. It gets much more difficult with a combination of backend and frontend development. This is just an easy fun way to get your feet wet.

I have always found AI very interesting, the fact that it is now possible to create an AI React app to amazing. As I mentioned before this is a very elementary way to incorporate AI although it is very neat. As a developer that is still in school I love coming across new challenges and obstacles. With this new information I learned I thought what better way to share it than with a blog. Now go and create an AI React app!!!

Resources- (alan.app/)

Items.Title: 使用 Solidity 进行 dApp 开发有哪些优势？ | What are the advantages of using Solidity for dApp development?
Items.Description:
在开发去中心化应用程序 (dApp) 时，选择正确的编程语言对于确保兼容性、效率和安全性至关重要。 我们来探讨一下 dApp 开发常用的编程语言、Solidity 的优势以及选择编程语言时需要考虑的因素。

它是一种专门为以太坊平台设计的静态类型、面向合约的语言。 以太坊兼容性：Solidity 是在以太坊区块链上开发 dApp 的主要语言。 它与以太坊虚拟机（EVM）无缝集成，可以直接访问以太坊的功能、库和工具。

智能合约开发：Solidity 允许开发人员定义和执行智能合约，这是 dApp 的重要组成部分。 它提供了合约继承、修改器、事件和数据结构等功能，以创建复杂且安全的智能合约开发。

行业采用：Solidity 已获得广泛的行业采用，使得更容易找到经验丰富的 Solidity 开发人员。 熟练的专业人员的可用性确保了高效的 dApp 开发和持续的支持。

虽然 Solidity 在基于以太坊的 dApp 中很流行，但其他编程语言也可用于不同区块链平台上的 dApp 开发。 一些著名的语言包括：

它通常用于构建 Substrate 等区块链平台，并可用于在这些平台上开发 dApp。

它提供简单性、性能和并发支持，使其适合构建去中心化应用程序。

在选择 dApp 开发的编程语言时，需要考虑以下几个因素：

平台兼容性：确保所选语言与目标区块链平台及其关联的虚拟机兼容，因为不同的平台可能有特定的语言要求。

开发者社区和生态系统：评估充满活力的开发者社区以及支持工具、库和框架的可用性。 这有助于获取资源、寻求帮助和利用现有解决方案。

安全注意事项：考虑语言的安全功能和编写安全代码的最佳实践。 特定于该语言的审核和测试工具可以帮助识别和减轻漏洞。

性能和可扩展性：评估语言的性能特征，特别是对于需要高吞吐量和可扩展性的 dApp。 提供高效资源利用并支持并行处理的语言可能是有利的。

互操作性：如果 dApp 需要与多个区块链或其他系统交互，请考虑促进互操作性的语言，并拥有支持跨链通信的库或框架。

总之，Solidity 广泛用于基于以太坊的 dApp 开发，提供与以太坊生态系统的兼容性、广泛的社区支持和强大的智能合约功能。 不过，根据区块链平台和项目需求，也可以考虑其他语言，如 JavaScript、Rust 和 Go。 在选择用于 dApp 开发的编程语言时，评估平台兼容性、开发者社区、安全功能、性能和互操作性至关重要。 Maticz, the leading dApp development service provider specializing in smart contracts and also a private blockchain development company , offers comprehensive services to cater to various dApp development needs. 原文:

When it comes to developing decentralized applications (dApps), choosing the right programming language is crucial for ensuring compatibility, efficiency, and security. Let’s explore the programming languages commonly used for dApp development, the advantages of Solidity, and the factors to consider when selecting a programming language.

One of the widely adopted languages for dApp development is Solidity. It is a statically-typed, contract-oriented language specifically designed for the Ethereum platform. Solidity offers several benefits for dApp development:

Ethereum Compatibility: Solidity is the primary language used for developing dApps on the Ethereum blockchain. It seamlessly integrates with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM), enabling direct access to Ethereum’s functionalities, libraries, and tools.

Smart Contract Development: Solidity allows developers to define and execute smart contracts, which are essential components of dApps. It provides features like contract inheritance, modifiers, events, and data structures to create complex and secure Smart Contract Development.

Industry Adoption: Solidity has gained significant industry adoption, making it easier to find experienced Solidity developers. This availability of skilled professionals ensures efficient dApp development and ongoing support.

While Solidity is popular for Ethereum-based dApps, other programming languages can be used for dApp development on different blockchain platforms. Some notable languages include:

– JavaScript: JavaScript is a widely-used programming language for web development and is often used in combination with frameworks like Node.js and React to build frontend interfaces for dApps.

– Rust: Rust is a systems programming language that offers memory safety, concurrency, and high performance. It is commonly used for building blockchain platforms like Substrate and can be utilized for developing dApps on those platforms.

– Go: Go is another language that has gained popularity in blockchain development. It provides simplicity, performance, and support for concurrency, making it suitable for building decentralized applications.

When choosing a programming language for dApp development, several factors need to be considered:

Platform Compatibility: Ensure that the chosen language is compatible with the targeted blockchain platform and its associated virtual machine, as different platforms may have specific language requirements.

Developer Community and Ecosystem: Evaluate the availability of a vibrant developer community and supporting tools, libraries, and frameworks. This helps in accessing resources, seeking assistance, and leveraging existing solutions.

Security Considerations: Consider the language’s security features and best practices for writing secure code. Auditing and testing tools specific to the language can aid in identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities.

Performance and Scalability: Assess the language’s performance characteristics, especially for dApps that require high throughput and scalability. Languages that offer efficient resource utilization and support parallel processing can be advantageous.

Interoperability: If the dApp needs to interact with multiple blockchains or other systems, consider languages that facilitate interoperability and have libraries or frameworks supporting cross-chain communication.

In conclusion, Solidity is widely used for Ethereum-based dApp development, providing compatibility with the Ethereum ecosystem, extensive community support, and robust smart contract capabilities. However, depending on the blockchain platform and project requirements, other languages like JavaScript, Rust, and Go can also be considered. When selecting a programming language for dApp development, it is crucial to evaluate platform compatibility, developer community, security features, performance, and interoperability. Items.Title: 如何在 NavLink 上为多个反应路线设置活动类 | How to set active class on NavLink for multiple react routes
Items.Description:
基兰·曼萨

发布于 7 月 5 日

我最近遇到了一个突出显示多条路线的导航链接的用例。

假设我们有两条路线

  1. 客户列表（/客户）。
  2. 客户详细信息 (/customer-details/:customerId)。

现在我有一个像这样的导航链接

<NavLink to={PATHS.CUSTOMERS}>Customers</Navlink>

该用例适用于两条路线，导航链路应处于活动状态。 这是我实现的逻辑：

 import { NavLink, matchPath, useLocation } from "react-router-dom"; export function NavigationBar() { const { pathname } = useLocation(); const setNavlinkClasses = (paths: string[]) => paths.some((path) => matchPath(path, pathname)) ? "navlink active" : "navlink"; return ( <nav> <NavLink to={PATHS.CUSTOMERS} className={setNavlinkClasses([PATHS.CUSTOMERS, PATHS.CUSTOMER_DETAILS])} >Customers</NavLink> </nav> ); }

就是这样。

希望有帮助😄
基兰👋👋

I recently got into one use case of highlighting the navlink for multiple routes.

The entry point in ui is customer listing and when you click on any record, we will be navigated to customer details page.

import { NavLink, matchPath, useLocation } from "react-router-dom"; export function NavigationBar() { const { pathname } = useLocation(); const setNavlinkClasses = (paths: string[]) => paths.some((path) => matchPath(path, pathname)) ? "navlink active" : "navlink"; return ( <nav> <NavLink to={PATHS.CUSTOMERS} className={setNavlinkClasses([PATHS.CUSTOMERS, PATHS.CUSTOMER_DETAILS])} >Customers</NavLink>
 </nav>
 );
}

And that's it.
